Essential competencies include proficiency with MS Excel and Chinese word processing, excellent interpersonal skills, and capacity for independent work. The role is distinctive in combining hands-on research execution with project coordination responsibilities across a community-based health initiative.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Logistics and Maritime Studies
description: This five-month Research Assistant position involves supporting a project investigating how maritime industry activities influence residents' awareness of maritime culture in the Greater Bay Area, utilizing large language models and panel data analysis. The role requires an honours degree or equivalent qualification and offers competitive remuneration. Applicants should contact the project leader for further details about specific responsibilities and application procedures.

title: Research Assistant (Full-time / Part-time)
department: Department of Building Environment and Energy Engineering
description: This full-time or part-time research assistant position involves supporting a project on sustainable food waste management using Black Soldier Fly bioconversion technology, with responsibilities including field measurements, bioconversion work, and machine learning modelling. Candidates must hold an honours degree in Engineering or Science, with demonstrated experience in food waste field measurements, bioconversion processes, and machine learning modelling, along with strong analytical and independent research capabilities. Bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (both written and spoken) is required. The appointment is for twelve months.

title: Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering
description: This position involves assisting with a research project focused on developing a platform for speech anonymization and deepfake detection to enhance voice security and privacy protection. Research Associates must hold a master's degree or strong honours degree in Electrical and Electronic Engineering, Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, or related fields with at least three years of research experience, while Research Assistants require an honours degree in these disciplines. All candidates must demonstrate strong interest in deepfake detection, voice security, and speech privacy, along with practical experience in Python or other scripting languages for system integration. The appointment is for a full-time or part-time twelve-month position within the Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering.

title: Project Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Project Associate / Research Assistant / Project Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: The Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering is recruiting for multiple levels of research positions (Project Fellow, Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant) on full-time or part-time basis for twelve-month appointments. All appointees will support a research project on cyber-physical systems for cross-border logistics hub synchronization in the Greater Bay Area. Qualifications range from doctoral degrees with 3+ years postdoctoral experience for Project Fellows down to bachelor's degrees for Research Assistants, with intermediate levels requiring master's degrees or honours degrees combined with relevant work experience. Interested candidates should contact the project leader for further details.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Language Science and Technology
description: This six-month postdoctoral position assists in a research project focused on scaling up a culturally responsive Chinese language education programme in kindergartens. Key responsibilities include designing evaluation tools, collecting and analysing project data, managing research outputs, and disseminating findings to stakeholders. Applicants must hold a doctoral degree in Chinese language, linguistics, or related fields with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, along with substantial experience in educational research and programme management. The ideal candidate will demonstrate passion for teaching, patience with children, and preferably have expertise in teaching Chinese as a second language, educational technology, or multimedia learning materials development.

title: Research Fellow / Project Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: The department is recruiting for five tiered research positions (Research Fellow, Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant) on a 24-month contract to support a project investigating creep age forming technology for 7050 aluminum aircraft panels. Candidates must hold relevant doctoral or master's degrees depending on the position level, with experience requirements ranging from entry-level (honours degree for Research Assistant) to senior postdoctoral researchers (three+ years experience). The roles involve assisting the project leader with research activities on advanced materials processing technology applicable to aerospace manufacturing. Applicants should contact the project coordinator for additional information about the specific position and requirements.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This posting offers full-time or part-time positions at three levels—Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant—in the Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering for a twelve-month appointment period. Appointees will support research into multimodal human-robot collaboration, focusing on few-shot perception and spatial skill learning with human-in-the-loop approaches. Required qualifications vary by position level: Postdoctoral Fellows need a doctoral degree with no more than five years post-qualification experience; Research Associates require a Master's degree or strong honours degree plus three or more years of relevant work experience; Research Assistants need an honours degree or equivalent. Competitive remuneration is offered for full-time positions.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: These full-time and part-time positions support research on vision-based human digital twin modelling and scene understanding for human-robot collaboration within the Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering. Three appointment levels are available: Postdoctoral Fellow (requiring a doctoral degree with up to five years post-qualification experience), Research Associate (requiring a Master's degree or honours degree with three or more years of relevant experience), and Research Assistant (requiring an honours degree). All positions are twelve-month contracts offering competitive remuneration for full-time roles.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: The Department of Mechanical Engineering is recruiting for full-time or part-time positions across three levels—Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant—to support a research project focused on developing ultrastrong, ductile, and thermally stable nanocrystalline alloys using novel nano-honeycomb architectural designs. Requirements vary by position level, ranging from a doctoral degree with minimal post-qualification experience for the Fellow role, to a master's or honours degree with three years of research experience for the Associate role, to an honours degree for the Assistant role. Appointments are for twelve months with competitive remuneration. Candidates should contact the project leader for further details.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position involves a 24-month appointment to support research on large language models applied to computational social science and fintech. Key responsibilities include identifying research challenges, collecting and processing data, developing machine learning frameworks, validating algorithms, and deploying language models on cloud servers. Candidates must hold a recent doctorate in information systems, computer science, applied mathematics, or statistics, with strong expertise in natural language processing and proficiency in Python, PyTorch, Linux, and Git. Strong communication skills in English and Chinese, combined with publications in deep learning or NLP, are essential, with preference given to those with high-quality peer-reviewed papers in these fields.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering
description: Two positions are available in the Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering to support research on high-Q microcavity-enhanced fluorescence spectroscopic optofluidic chips for detecting water pollutants. The Postdoctoral Fellow role (6-24 months) requires a doctoral degree in optical, electronic, or electrical engineering with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, while the Research Associate position (3-12 months, full-time or part-time) requires a master's degree or strong honours degree with at least three years of relevant research experience. Both appointees will assist the project leader in developing advanced detection technologies for water quality applications. Interested candidates should contact the project supervisor for further information.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Logistics and Maritime Studies
description: The Department of Logistics and Maritime Studies is recruiting for three tiers of research positions (Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, or Research Assistant) on a full-time or part-time basis for six to twelve months to support a project on optimization for logistics management. Applicants must have relevant academic qualifications ranging from an honours degree to a doctoral degree depending on the position level, with the Postdoctoral Fellow role capped at five years post-qualification experience and the Research Associate role requiring at least three years of research experience. A distinctive requirement across all positions is hands-on experience in optimization algorithm design and implementation. Interested candidates should contact the project leader directly for further information.

title: Part-time Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: These are twelve-month part-time positions in industrial and systems engineering supporting research on rapid reconfiguration of human-robot collaborative assembly lines for smart manufacturing. Three appointment levels are available: Postdoctoral Fellow (requiring a doctoral degree with no more than five years post-qualification experience), Research Associate (requiring a Master's degree or strong honours degree with three or more years relevant experience), and Research Assistant (requiring an honours degree). Appointees will assist the project leader in developing methodologies to enable human-centric manufacturing systems that can quickly adapt robotic assembly configurations.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Building Environment and Energy Engineering
description: This twelve-month Research Assistant position involves supporting a policy research project examining Legal Entity Identifier and electronic authentication frameworks across Guangdong, Hong Kong, and Macao, including analysis of their effectiveness and societal responses. Candidates must hold an honours degree in Engineering or equivalent qualification. The role offers competitive remuneration and requires candidates to assist the project leader in conducting comprehensive policy research and stakeholder engagement across the three regions.

title: Research Assistant
description: This three-month research assistant position involves supporting a project on neuromorphic multi-paradigm learning that integrates biological plasticity mechanisms. Candidates must hold an honours degree and demonstrate strong technical competency in PyTorch programming, spiking neural networks, and familiarity with brain-inspired learning methods and large language models. The role is distinctive for its focus on bridging neuroscience and artificial intelligence through neuromorphic computing approaches. A competitive remuneration package is offered.

title: Research Assistant
description: This six-month research assistant position involves supporting the development of an autonomous kitchen assistant and robotic cooking system within the biomedical engineering department. Candidates must hold an honours degree in Computer Science or a related field and demonstrate proficiency in software engineering. The role offers competitive remuneration and appeals to those interested in robotics and automation applied to domestic environments.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate (Full-time/Part-time) / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering
description: The Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering seeks to fill three related positions (Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant) on contracts ranging from three to twenty-four months. Appointees will support research on ultra-sensitive optical fiber-tip optomechanical sensors designed for gas sensing applications in electronic nose technology. Requirements vary by level: Postdoctoral Fellows need a doctoral degree with no more than five years post-qualification experience, Research Associates require a master's degree or strong honours degree in engineering with at least three years of relevant experience, and Research Assistants need a relevant honours degree. The role offers both full-time and part-time options with flexible contract lengths.

title: Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
department: Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering
description: The department seeks to fill three tiered research positions (Research Fellow, Postdoctoral Fellow, or Research Associate) for three to twenty-four month appointments to support a project investigating complex antenna systems operating in the sub-10GHz frequency band. Research Fellows require a doctoral degree plus three years of postdoctoral experience, while Postdoctoral Fellows need a doctorate with no more than five years of post-qualification experience; Research Associates require a master's degree or strong honours degree with three years of relevant work experience and demonstrated expertise in BLE system design, AI data analysis, and mechanical system design. All candidates must demonstrate strong English proficiency for literature review, data reporting, and communication. Applicants should contact the project leader for additional information.

title: Part-time Project Associate / Project Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This is an eight-month part-time position in the Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering to support a project on engineering ethics for emerging technologies. The appointee will assist the project leader in executing research activities and organizing project-related events. The role requires either a Master's degree or strong honours degree with three or more years of research experience for the Associate level, or an honours degree for the Assistant level, with preference given to candidates with established connections to secondary schools. The hourly rates are HK$178 for Project Associates and HK$125 for Project Assistants.

title: Research Associate
department: Department of Aeronautical and Aviation Engineering
description: This is a twelve-month Research Associate position in aeronautical and aviation engineering focused on assisting with a project on modelling and optimizing low-altitude transport systems for logistics and passenger applications. The role requires a doctoral degree or equivalent qualification. The position offers competitive remuneration and applicants should contact the listed project leader for additional information, with applications due by 15 May 2026.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This seven-month position in Industrial and Systems Engineering involves assisting the project leader in research on methodologies for rapid reconfiguration of human-robot collaborative assembly production lines in smart manufacturing. The role is offered at three levels—Postdoctoral Fellow (requiring a doctorate and maximum five years post-qualification experience), Research Associate (requiring a Master's degree or strong honours degree with three or more years of research experience), or Research Assistant (requiring an honours degree). The position offers flexibility in qualifications and experience levels, allowing applicants to match their background to the appropriate role tier.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Project Associate / Project Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: Three fixed-term positions (twelve-month appointments) are available in the Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering, offering full-time or part-time arrangements at three levels: Postdoctoral Fellow, Project Associate, and Project Assistant. All positions involve assisting with research on an indoor biomedical equipment locator system. Requirements vary by level: Postdoctoral Fellows must hold a doctoral degree with no more than five years post-qualification experience; Project Associates need a Master's degree or strong honours degree plus three or more years of relevant research experience; Project Assistants require an honours degree or equivalent. Interested candidates should contact the supervising professor for further details.

title: Research Assistant
description: This is a six to twelve-month research assistant position in the Department of Computing focused on developing an AI-powered learning assistant system designed for flipped classroom environments. The role involves supporting the project leader with research and development work that requires hands-on experience in Unity or Web development, digital avatar creation, and large language model solutions. Candidates must hold an honours degree or equivalent qualification and demonstrate genuine interest in human-computer interaction and e-learning research. A competitive salary package is offered, with applications closing on 5 May 2026.

title: Principal Research Fellow / Senior Research Fellow / Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant / Research Technical Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
description: The Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering seeks to fill multiple positions across seven levels—from Principal Research Fellow to Research Technical Assistant—to support research on smart railway technology and applications within a national research center's Hong Kong branch. Appointments are available on a full-time or part-time basis for six-month terms, with three posts at each level. Qualifications range from doctoral degrees with substantial postdoctoral experience (8+ years for Principal Fellows) down to higher diplomas for technical assistants, with corresponding requirements for research experience at each tier. All appointees will assist the research director in advancing railway electrification, automation, and related technological innovation.

title: Research Technical Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Aeronautical and Aviation Engineering
description: This position offers full-time or part-time research support for a nine-month project investigating adaptive safety requirements in autonomous vehicle localization systems within Hong Kong. Candidates must hold a higher diploma or equivalent qualification in a relevant discipline with at least three years of post-qualification experience. The role involves assisting the project leader with research activities, with full-time positions offering competitive salaries and part-time work compensated at HK$120 per hour. Applications close on 6 March 2026.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Aeronautical and Aviation Engineering
description: This is a twelve-month postdoctoral position supporting research on detonation propagation in curved channels within aeronautical and aviation engineering. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience and possess strong expertise in combustion, detonation, or fluid dynamics within mechanical or aerospace engineering. Essential qualifications include demonstrated experience in computational fluid dynamics, experimental and/or numerical methods for studying detonation phenomena, and a track record of publishing in high-impact international journals. The role is distinctive for its specialization in detonation research, requiring both computational and experimental competencies applied to a focused engineering problem.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This is a twelve-month postdoctoral position in industrial and systems engineering focused on assisting with research into self-healing reconstruction technology for snake robot formation. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree and have no more than five years of post-qualification experience. The role offers competitive remuneration, with applications closing on 26 June 2026.

title: Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This Research Assistant position in Industrial and Systems Engineering offers full-time or part-time opportunities to support a research project on hybrid energy field assisted ultra-precision diamond turning for a twelve-month appointment period. Candidates must hold an honours degree or equivalent qualification and will assist the project leader with research activities. Full-time positions offer competitive remuneration, while part-time roles are compensated at HK$125 per hour. The distinctive focus is on advanced manufacturing technology combining energy fields with precision diamond turning processes.

title: Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: The Department of Mechanical Engineering seeks to fill multiple research positions (Research Fellow, Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant) on a full-time or part-time basis for a 24-month appointment period. Appointees will support a research project focused on sonic metagrating barriers for broadband sound absorption and wave manipulation. Candidates must possess relevant qualifications ranging from an honours degree (for Research Assistant roles) to a doctoral degree with postdoctoral experience (for Research Fellow positions), along with demonstrated knowledge of acoustics, noise measurement, or vibration. The positions offer opportunities to contribute to an acoustics-focused research initiative within mechanical engineering.

title: Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: The Department of Mechanical Engineering seeks Research Fellows, Postdoctoral Fellows, Research Associates, and Research Assistants (full-time or part-time, 24-month appointments) to support a project developing aural-comfortable headsets for people with hearing sensitivities and hearing loss. Candidates must possess expertise in acoustics, noise measurement, or vibration, with qualifications ranging from honours degree (for Research Assistants) to doctoral degree plus three years' postdoctoral experience (for Research Fellows). The appointees will work as a team assisting the project leader in the research and development phases of this specialized audio technology initiative.

title: Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: The Department of Mechanical Engineering is recruiting for three tiered research positions (Research Fellow, Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant) on a full-time or part-time basis for a 24-month appointment. All appointees will support a project focused on designing and modeling a sway structural frame that integrates flow control, broadband noise reduction, and vibration suppression. Requirements vary by level, ranging from a doctoral degree plus three years of postdoctoral experience for Research Fellows to an honours degree for Research Assistants, with all candidates needing demonstrated knowledge in aerodynamics, acoustics, or vibration. Interested applicants should contact the project leader for further details.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant
description: This posting covers three tiered positions within Mechanical Engineering, each offering a twelve-month appointment to support a research project on sustainable alkali and acid production from seawater for concrete recycling applications. The Postdoctoral Fellow role requires a doctoral degree with no more than five years post-qualification experience, while the Research Associate position requires a master's degree or strong honours degree plus three years of relevant research experience, and the Research Assistant role requires an honours degree. All applicants must demonstrate practical experience in electrochemical energy systems and will be offered competitive remuneration.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Part-time Project Technical Assistant
department: Department of Aeronautical and Aviation Engineering
description: This position involves postdoctoral and part-time technical assistant roles supporting research on 3D geometric feature computation for large aircraft using point cloud techniques and advanced 3D perception algorithms. Appointees will conduct research on segmentation, localization, and mapping; develop and implement algorithms; design validation experiments; and contribute to publications and conference presentations while collaborating with multidisciplinary teams and industry partners. The postdoctoral fellow requires a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, while technical assistants need an honours degree, with both requiring strong backgrounds in 3D perception, proficiency in Python and C++, independent research capability, and problem-solving skills. The postdoctoral position is initially for six months with possible extension, while technical assistant roles are each for ten months.

title: Research Fellow / Project Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate (Full-time/Part-time) / Research Assistant / Project Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: The department is recruiting across four tiers of research positions (twelve total posts) for a 24-month project on metal forming technologies and numerical modelling, where appointees will assist the project leader. Requirements vary by level: Research Fellows/Project Fellows need a doctorate plus three years of postdoctoral experience; Postdoctoral Fellows require a doctorate with no more than five years' post-qualification experience; Research Associates need a master's degree or strong honours degree with three years of relevant experience; and Research Assistants need an honours degree or equivalent. All candidates must demonstrate strong expertise in materials processing technologies, particularly light alloys and energy materials, with preference for those possessing knowledge of computer programming, artificial intelligence, or machine learning.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: The position involves supporting a research project on vision-based human digital twin modeling and scene understanding for human-robot collaboration, with three appointment levels available. The Postdoctoral Fellow position requires a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, while the Research Associate position requires a Master's degree or honors degree with at least three years of relevant experience. The Research Assistant position requires an honors degree or equivalent. All positions are for twelve-month appointments and involve assisting the project leader in research and related tasks.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: The Department of Mechanical Engineering seeks a Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, or Research Assistant (full-time or part-time) for a twelve-month appointment to support research on ultrastrong-yet-ductile refractory high-entropy alloys with coherent nanoprecipitates. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree (for Postdoctoral Fellow, with ≤5 years post-qualification experience), master's/honours degree with ≥3 years research experience (for Research Associate), or honours degree (for Research Assistant). The role involves assisting the project leader with research activities on atomic structure, stability, and deformation mechanisms of advanced materials. Competitive remuneration is offered for full-time positions.

title: Project Fellow / Project Associate
department: Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
description: This twelve-month position supports a research project on intelligent tropical-storm-resilient systems for coastal cities, requiring the appointee to provide administrative and technical assistance to the project leader. The Project Fellow role requires a doctoral degree with at least three years of postdoctoral experience, while the Project Associate role requires a Master's degree or honours degree with three years of relevant research or work experience. Candidates should have experience in climate-resilient infrastructure or large AI models, strong bilingual English-Chinese communication skills, excellent interpersonal abilities, and proficiency in standard office software and Chinese word processing. The ideal candidate will be self-motivated, capable of independent work, and able to function effectively as a team member.

title: Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: A twelve-month Research Assistant position in Industrial and Systems Engineering is available on a full-time or part-time basis to support a project on magnetic field-assisted ultra-precision grinding of nickel-based superalloys. The role requires an honours degree or equivalent qualification and involves assisting the project leader with research activities. Full-time appointments offer competitive remuneration, while part-time positions pay HK$125 per hour. Applications close on 28 May 2026.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate (Full-time/Part-time) / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This posting advertises six positions across three levels within the Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering for a 12-18 month research project focused on sustainable campus lighting and charging infrastructure with flexible multi-source power generation. Postdoctoral Fellows must hold a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, Research Associates require a Master's degree or strong honours degree with at least three years of relevant research experience, and Research Assistants need an honours degree or equivalent qualification. All appointees will support the project leader in conducting research on integrated renewable energy management systems for campus infrastructure. The positions offer flexibility in employment arrangements, with Research Associates and Assistants available as either full-time or part-time roles.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate (Full-time/Part-time) / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: The Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering is recruiting for multiple positions—four postdoctoral fellows, two research associates (full-time or part-time), and two research assistants (full-time or part-time)—on contracts of twelve to eighteen months. All appointees will support research on developing low-cost, vibration-durable in-wheel traction motors for next-generation electric vehicles. Postdoctoral fellows must hold a doctorate with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, research associates require a master's degree or strong honours degree plus three years of relevant experience, and research assistants need an honours degree or equivalent qualification.

title: Part-time Research Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This part-time research assistant position involves supporting a project leader on a twelve-month research initiative focused on hybrid 3D printing and nanoimprinting of ceramics for device casings, combining biomimetic design with sustainable manufacturing practices. Applicants must hold an honours degree or equivalent qualification, with preference given to candidates with laser technology experience. The role is compensated at HK$125 per hour, with applications closing on 10 April 2026.

title: Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: The Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering seeks one Research Fellow and three Postdoctoral Fellows (each for twelve-month appointments) to assist with developing an intelligent ultra-precision air-bearing hydrostatic spindle for semiconductor wafer grinding. Research Fellow candidates must hold a doctoral degree plus at least three years of postdoctoral research experience, while Postdoctoral Fellow applicants require a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience. Positions offer competitive remuneration packages, with applications due by 27 April 2026.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: The Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering seeks to fill three positions—Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant—each for twelve months, to support a research project on optimizing optical design for enhanced visual experiences in virtual and augmented reality headsets. Postdoctoral Fellows must hold a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, Research Associates require a Master's degree or strong honours degree with at least three years of relevant experience, and Research Assistants need an honours degree or equivalent. All appointees will assist the project leader in conducting the research. Interested applicants should contact the listed project supervisor for further details.

title: Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant
description: The Department of Mechanical Engineering is recruiting for four tiered research positions (Research Fellow, Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant) on twelve-month contracts to support a project on the design and net-shape forming of micro-/meso-scaled surface functional structures. Requirements range from an honours degree for Research Assistants to a doctoral degree plus three years of postdoctoral experience for Research Fellows, with intermediate positions requiring master's degrees or doctorates with limited postdoctoral experience. All appointees will work under a project leader to advance research in precision manufacturing and materials processing. Interested candidates should contact the project supervisor for additional information.

title: Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant
description: The Department of Mechanical Engineering seeks researchers at multiple levels (Research Fellow, Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant) to support a six-month project on adaptive 3D printing encompassing design, manufacturing, modeling, and optimization. Qualifications range from doctoral degrees with three years of postdoctoral experience for the most senior role to honours degree holders for the assistant position, with intermediate positions requiring doctorates or master's degrees paired with relevant research experience. All appointees will work under a project leader to advance research in advanced manufacturing technologies. Interested candidates should contact the project supervisor for additional details.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: This position offers full-time or part-time roles at three levels (Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, or Research Assistant) within the Department of Mechanical Engineering for an initial appointment of three to twelve months, renewable. Appointees will support research on hybrid neural-physical solvers for biomedical simulations that leverage artificial intelligence to accelerate computational solutions. Candidates must demonstrate strong mathematical and coding abilities with a solid mechanics background, with specific requirements varying by level: doctoral degree for Postdoctoral Fellows (within five years post-qualification), master's degree or strong honours degree with three years' experience for Research Associates, or honours degree for Research Assistants. All applicants should be highly self-motivated and can contact the project leader for additional information.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: The Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering is recruiting both a Postdoctoral Fellow and Research Assistant for three-month fixed-term positions to support a research project on multi-sensor-based smart sorting systems for waste recycling and sustainable development. Candidates for the Postdoctoral Fellow role must hold a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-doctoral experience, while Research Assistant applicants need an honours degree or equivalent. Both positions require hands-on research experience in ultra-precision machining, precision metrology, smart defect detection, and smart testing and inspection, with competitive remuneration offered.

title: Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This position offers full-time or part-time research assistant roles within the Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering, with flexible appointment periods ranging from one to twelve months. Appointees will support a project leader on research involving magnetohydrodynamics-assisted ultra-precision diamond cutting for creating nano-patterned metallic surfaces. Applicants must hold an honours degree or equivalent qualification. The role offers competitive remuneration for full-time positions or HK$125 per hour for part-time work, with applications closing on 13 March 2026.

title: Research Assistant / Part-time Research Assistant
description: The School of Design seeks four research assistants (two full-time for three months, two part-time for six months) to support an Extended Reality and gamified urban furniture design project. Key responsibilities include developing Mixed Reality content in Unity for HoloLens, integrating gamification with Rhino3D tools, analyzing trans-disciplinary research, creating design frameworks, and mentoring students on platform use. Candidates must hold a master's degree in Architecture, Computer Science, or Engineering, with demonstrated expertise in XR software, gamified interactive content creation, and strong English communication skills, plus the ability to work independently and maintain project schedules.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Land Surveying and Geo-Informatics
description: This postdoctoral position involves supporting a research project focused on remote sensing applications to urban climate, ecosystems, and the environment, with emphasis on geospatial artificial intelligence. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in a relevant field such as Remote Sensing, GIS, Computer Science, AI, or related disciplines, with no more than five years of post-doctoral experience, and demonstrate expertise in algorithm development, scientific computing (Python/R), and geospatial analysis. The role requires strong quantitative and computational skills, with preference given to applicants experienced in AI, cloud computing, and high-performance data processing. The appointment is fixed-term, ranging from twelve to thirty-six months, and requires demonstrated publication record and the ability to work both independently and within an interdisciplinary team.

title: Head
department: Department of Public and International Affairs
description: This position seeks a Head to lead the Department of Public and International Affairs, reporting to the Provost through the Dean of the College of Liberal Arts and Social Sciences. The role requires providing strategic leadership in research and professional education while promoting academic excellence and advancing the university's mission and vision. Candidates must possess a doctoral degree, a distinguished record of research and teaching, substantial tertiary education experience, and strong management, communication, and networking capabilities. The appointment is offered on a concurrent basis for approximately three years at an academic rank and competitive remuneration commensurate with qualifications and experience.

title: Head
deadline: Applications will be considered until the position(s) is/are filled
description: This headship position leads the Department of Chinese and History within the College of Liberal Arts and Social Sciences, reporting to the Provost and Deputy President. The Head is responsible for providing strategic leadership in research and professional education, fostering academic excellence, and aligning departmental activities with the university's strategic vision of globally recognized professional education and impactful research. The ideal candidate must demonstrate a distinguished record of research, teaching, and scholarship with substantial tertiary education experience, alongside strong management capabilities, teamwork commitment, and excellent communication and networking skills. A doctoral degree with strong academic qualifications is required; the appointment is concurrent and initially for three years, with remuneration commensurate with qualifications and performance.

title: Head
deadline: Applications will be considered until the position(s) is/are filled
description: This position seeks a Head of Department of Marketing to provide strategic leadership for the marketing department within a dynamic, internationally-oriented university. The successful candidate must demonstrate a distinguished record in research, teaching, and scholarship, with substantial tertiary education experience, strong management capabilities, and excellent communication skills to foster internal and external relationships. The role requires a doctoral degree and reports to the Provost through the Dean of the College of Business, with responsibility for advancing academic excellence, research initiatives, and aligning departmental goals with institutional strategy. The appointment is concurrent for approximately three years, with compensation and rank commensurate with qualifications and experience.

title: Head
deadline: Applications will be considered until the position(s) is/are filled
description: This role seeks a Head to lead the Department of Media and Communication, reporting to the Provost through the Dean of the College of Liberal Arts and Social Sciences. The successful candidate will provide strategic direction in research and professional education while promoting academic excellence and aligning departmental initiatives with the university's strategic vision. Requirements include a doctoral degree, a distinguished record in research and teaching at the tertiary level, proven management effectiveness, and strong communication skills to build internal and external partnerships. The appointment is concurrent for an initial three-year term, with remuneration and academic rank determined by qualifications and experience.

title: Head
deadline: Applications will be considered until the position(s) is/are filled
description: This position invites applications for a Head of the Department of Economics and Finance, reporting to the Provost through the Dean of College of Business. The successful candidate will provide strategic leadership in research and professional education, fostering academic excellence and advancing the department's alignment with institutional goals. Required qualifications include a doctoral degree, a distinguished record of research and teaching, substantial tertiary education experience, strong management capabilities, and excellent communication and networking skills. The appointment is concurrent for approximately three years at an academic rank and competitive salary commensurate with qualifications and experience.

title: Head
deadline: Applications will be considered until the position(s) is/are filled
description: This position seeks a Head to lead the Department of Biomedical Sciences, reporting to the Provost through the Dean of the College of Biomedicine, with responsibility for providing strategic direction in research and professional education while promoting academic excellence and advancing the university's mission. The successful candidate must demonstrate a distinguished research record, substantial tertiary education experience, outstanding management capability, and strong communication skills to build internal and external partnerships. A doctoral degree with strong academic and professional qualifications is essential. The appointment is offered on a concurrent basis for approximately three years at an academic rank commensurate with qualifications and experience, with a competitive remuneration package based on market rates and individual performance.

title: Director of Human Resources
deadline: Applications will be considered until the position(s) is/are filled
description: The Director of Human Resources will lead the institution's entire HR function across academic, research, and administrative staff, developing strategic people initiatives aligned with the university's mission and supporting organizational change. Key responsibilities include overseeing talent acquisition, performance management, compensation, employee relations, HR governance, and digital transformation of HR processes while ensuring compliance with Hong Kong employment regulations. The ideal candidate brings substantial senior HR leadership experience from complex organizations such as higher education or large corporations, with demonstrated expertise in leading organizational change, understanding Hong Kong employment law, and implementing modern, people-centered practices. This role requires strategic partnership with senior leadership to align human capital with the institution's research excellence and professional education objectives.

title: Director of Finance
deadline: Applications will be considered until the position(s) is/are filled
description: This position leads the Finance Office as a senior member of the university's management team, with responsibility for developing financial policies and overseeing accounting services, budget planning, compliance, procurement, payroll, treasury, and investment management. The successful candidate must hold a Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or Business Management with relevant professional accounting qualifications and extensive senior-level financial management experience, ideally within higher education. The role requires strategic vision, strong leadership, sound business judgment, and the ability to provide professional advice supporting the university's development initiatives. A competitive remuneration package and comprehensive benefits are offered on a fixed-term contract basis.

title: Head
deadline: Applications will be considered until the position(s) is/are filled
description: This position invites applications for the Head of the Department of Neuroscience, reporting to the Provost through the Dean of the College of Biomedicine. The Head will provide strategic leadership in research and professional education, promoting academic excellence and advancing the department's alignment with the university's broader mission and vision. Candidates must hold a PhD and/or MD/DVM, demonstrate a distinguished record in neuroscience research, teaching, and scholarship, and possess substantial tertiary education experience alongside strong management capabilities and communication skills. The appointment is concurrent for an initial three-year term with salary and benefits commensurate with qualifications and experience.

title: Head
department: Department of Infectious Diseases and Public Health
deadline: Applications will be considered until the position(s) is/are filled
description: This position seeks a Head to lead the Department of Infectious Diseases and Public Health, reporting to senior university leadership through a college dean. The role requires providing strategic direction across research, teaching, and professional education while promoting academic excellence and advancing the university's mission and vision. Candidates should hold a PhD and veterinary degree (DVM, BVSc, or equivalent) with an outstanding track record in teaching, research, and academic management, along with strong leadership and communication skills to develop internal and external relationships. Exceptional candidates from non-veterinary backgrounds will be considered, and appointment will be made at an academic rank appropriate to the candidate's qualifications.

title: Head
deadline: Applications will be considered until the position(s) is/are filled
description: This position seeks a Head to lead the Department of Veterinary Clinical Sciences, reporting to the Dean through the Provost. The successful candidate will direct research, teaching, and clinical services across the university's veterinary medical centre and diagnostic laboratory while advancing academic excellence and the institution's strategic mission. Required qualifications include a PhD, veterinary qualification recognised for practice in Hong Kong (DVM, BVSc, or equivalent), and board certification as a veterinary specialist; candidates must also demonstrate a distinguished record in research, teaching, and tertiary education management. The role is distinctive in combining academic leadership with operational oversight of clinical facilities and diagnostic services, requiring both scholarly excellence and substantial administrative expertise.

title: Part-time Lecturers
department: College of Liberal Arts and Social Sciences
deadline: Applications will be considered until the position(s) is/are filled
description: Four part-time lecturer positions are available to teach postgraduate courses in a Master's programme on Cross Sectoral Leadership, with instruction delivered in Chinese. Courses include Research Design and Methods, Innovative Finance for Global Leaders, Social Entrepreneurship, and related subjects, with teaching scheduled during daytime, evening, and weekend hours throughout the academic year. Applicants must hold a relevant Master's degree with related professional experience, with preference given to those with university teaching or industrial experience in the field. Compensation is competitive and commensurate with qualifications and experience.

title: Research Assistant Professor
department: Department of Geography and Resource Management
description: This Research Assistant Professor position focuses on wetland ecology and biogeochemistry, specifically investigating coastal wetlands as climate solutions. Candidates must hold a PhD in physical geography, environmental science, or related fields, with strong expertise in biogeochemistry, ecology, soil science, or geospatial analysis, along with a proven publication record in top-tier journals and excellent communication skills. The role involves conducting independent and collaborative research, securing external funding as Principal Investigator, publishing peer-reviewed work, and contributing to teaching and student supervision. The appointment is contract-based for up to three years, renewable based on performance and funding availability.

description: This Lecturer position in the Office of University General Education involves teaching two core courses on China and Hong Kong within a General Education programme, with opportunities to teach additional China-related courses. The ideal candidate holds a Ph.D. in Humanities or Social Sciences with expertise in China, though backgrounds in economic development or international relations are particularly valued; relevant advantages include research credentials on China topics, fieldwork experience in China, or prior China studies teaching experience. The role encompasses curriculum development, enhancement of teaching and learning outcomes, and support for student and academic enrichment activities. The appointment is initially contract-based for one to two years beginning August 2026 or January 2027, with renewal dependent on satisfactory performance.

description: The Department of Mathematics invites applications for non-substantive Research Assistant Professor positions across all mathematical disciplines, with appointments beginning August 2026 on three-year renewable contracts. Candidates must hold a PhD in mathematics or related fields and demonstrate strong research potential and teaching capability, with primary focus on independent research and minimal teaching responsibilities (one course per year). The department is a highly ranked international institution seeking promising early-career mathematicians whose research aligns with existing faculty expertise. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis from December 1, 2025 onwards, requiring a CV, academic credentials, and references from at least four referees (three research, one teaching).

Candidates must hold a PhD in mathematics or a related field and demonstrate an established research record with publications alongside a commitment to teaching excellence. The initial appointment is contract-based for up to three years beginning August 2026, with the possibility of substantiation or longer-term employment thereafter. The department is highly internationally ranked and seeks to recruit accomplished scholars to strengthen its reputation in both research and education.

The successful candidate will teach at undergraduate and postgraduate levels while contributing to departmental development initiatives and community engagement. The fixed-term position, renewable based on performance, begins in August 2026 and offers an opportunity to join a diverse, internationally-recognised department ranked 18th globally.

description: This position invites distinguished scholars at various professoriate levels to join the Department of Educational Administration and Policy, which is a leading international center for education research and professional training. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in educational administration, leadership, or a related field, with a strong research background, a track record of publications, and demonstrated excellence in teaching. The successful appointee(s) will teach undergraduate and postgraduate courses, supervise graduate students, lead research initiatives, secure external competitive funding, and may assume leadership roles in research centers. department: Department of Educational Administration and Policy
description: The Department of Educational Administration and Policy seeks distinguished scholars at professor, associate professor, or assistant professor levels to lead teaching and research in education foundations and policy studies. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in relevant disciplines (policy studies, early childhood education, ethics, sociology/philosophy of education, AI in education, or related fields) and demonstrate strong research productivity, publication records, and potential for securing competitive grants. Appointees will teach both undergraduate and postgraduate courses, supervise graduate students, pursue external funding, and contribute to departmental administration. This is an initial three-year contract position that may lead to longer-term appointment or substantiation, and requires demonstrated commitment to research excellence and academic leadership at the international level (particularly for senior ranks).

title: Research Assistant Professor
description: This Research Assistant Professor position in translational auditory neuroscience offers early-career researchers a three-year, funded opportunity to develop an independent research profile under mentorship, with potential progression to tenure-track status based on performance. The role requires a PhD in a relevant discipline (neuroscience, engineering, audiology, psychology, or biomedical science) and strong technical competencies including proficiency in Python or MATLAB, signal processing, and data science, alongside demonstrated ability to conduct experiments with both human participants and animal models. The position is distinctive in providing access to dual research environments—fundamental animal-based research at the neuroscience institute and patient-facing clinical research at affiliated hospitals—with minimal teaching obligations to prioritize research development. Ideal candidates will have experience in electrophysiological measurements, psychoacoustic assessments, or functional imaging, combined with excellent communication skills and the self-motivation to progress toward an independent principal investigator role.

title: Professor / Associate Professor / Assistant Professor (Hospitality and Tourism)
department: School of Hotel and Tourism Management
description: The School of Hotel and Tourism Management seeks tenure-track or tenured faculty at all ranks (Assistant, Associate, or Professor) to conduct high-quality research in hospitality and tourism, publish in top-tier journals, and teach courses at undergraduate and postgraduate levels. Applicants must hold a PhD in hospitality and tourism or a related field, with Assistant Professor positions initially offered on three-year contracts potentially leading to longer-term appointments. For Associate and Professor ranks, candidates should preferably have industry experience, expertise in programme development and executive education, and a demonstrated track record in grants, contracts, and academic leadership. The position offers the distinctive opportunity to contribute to one of Asia's pioneering hospitality and tourism programmes, including an integrated BBA and unique MSc in Real Estate, both internationally accredited.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
description: This Project Co-ordinator II role supports the Centre for Innovation and Technology within the Faculty of Engineering, serving as secretary to the Centre Director and primary liaison with internal and external stakeholders. Key responsibilities include editing and proofreading publications and website content, coordinating events and marketing communications, and providing administrative support for major Centre initiatives. The ideal candidate holds a Bachelor's degree or higher in Marketing, Communications, Public Relations, or related field, with at least 3 years of relevant work experience, fluent written and spoken English and Chinese proficiency, and strong organisational and interpersonal skills. The position is offered on an initial 2-year renewable contract, with prior tertiary institution experience considered advantageous.

title: Executive Officer II
department: Department of Otorhinolaryngology, Head and Neck Surgery
description: This Executive Officer II role in the Division of Speech Therapy requires managing postgraduate and professional diploma programmes, overseeing divisional administration including budgeting and human resources, and organizing academic events while providing secretarial support. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree, have at least three years of post-qualification experience preferably in academic administration or project coordination at tertiary institutions, and demonstrate fluency in written and spoken English and Chinese (including Putonghua) alongside strong analytical, communication, and multitasking abilities. Essential qualifications include proficiency in MS Office applications, excellent problem-solving skills, and a self-motivated, responsible work approach, with applicants required to submit evidence of language and academic credentials or face rejection. The position offers an initial one-year contract renewable based on performance, with shortlisted candidates required to complete a written assessment.

title: Clerical Assistant
description: This clerical assistant position in the Estates Management Office involves providing administrative and operational support, maintaining records, responding to inquiries, and performing related duties as needed. Candidates must have completed secondary education, strong word-processing and MS Office skills, fluency in English and Chinese, meticulous attention to detail, and excellent communication abilities, with SAP system experience being advantageous. The role includes a computer skills assessment for shortlisted applicants. The position is offered on an initial two-year renewable contract with performance-based continuation.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
description: This Project Co-ordinator II role supports a residential college within a university, managing admissions, scholarships, financial aid, and exchange programmes while coordinating student activities and serving as secretary to college committees. The ideal candidate holds a bachelor's degree or higher with at least three years of post-qualification experience, ideally in tertiary education, and must demonstrate strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, computer literacy, and excellent interpersonal skills. Prior experience with student admissions, scholarships, or college exchange programmes is highly valued. The position requires occasional work outside standard office hours and is initially offered on a two-year renewable contract basis.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
department: Centre for the Studies of Daoist Culture
description: This position supports administrative and financial operations at a centre specializing in Daoist cultural studies, with key responsibilities including coordinating programs, research activities, conferences, and public lectures; preparing proposals and reports; and providing secretarial support to the Centre Director. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Chinese Culture or related fields, possess at least two years of administrative experience preferably in tertiary institutions, and demonstrate fluency in both English and Chinese alongside proficiency in computer applications. The role requires independent working ability, strong organisational and interpersonal skills, with prior research project coordination experience being advantageous. Appointments are initially made on renewable two-year contracts with performance-based continuation.

title: Project Assistant II
description: The position is a Project Assistant II in the Department of Psychiatry, primarily based at Tai Po Hospital, responsible for providing administrative, secretarial, and operational support to the department. Key duties include coordinating logistical support for student activities and examinations, organizing academic events such as workshops and conferences, managing website and social media content, and compiling data for document preparation. Candidates must have completed secondary education with Grade E/Level 2 or above in at least five subjects (including English and Chinese), along with strong proficiency in MS Office applications, bilingual communication skills, and the ability to work independently under pressure. The role is offered on a contract basis for up to two years with the possibility of renewal, with shortlisted candidates required to pass an IT proficiency test.

title: Senior Editor
description: The Senior Editor will drive content creation for the Communications and Public Relations Office, overseeing coordination of content generation across the department while writing, editing, and proofreading articles to ensure effective message delivery aligned with institutional priorities. Candidates must hold at least a Bachelor's degree (Master's preferred) and demonstrate 12+ years of post-qualification editorial experience in media or publication, combined with fluent written and spoken English and Chinese including Putonghua. The role requires strong communication skills to engage multiple stakeholders and knowledge of higher education and the public sector in Hong Kong and mainland China, with background in journalism, communication, or social sciences preferred. The position is offered on an initial 2-year renewable contract, subject to performance review.

title: Research Associate(s)
department: Department of Systems Engineering and Engineering Management
description: This role involves developing and delivering AI education initiatives across Hong Kong schools through designing curriculum materials, conducting workshops, and training both teachers and students in artificial intelligence concepts. Candidates must hold a Master's degree with at least four years of relevant experience, preferably in AI, computer science, engineering, education, or related fields, alongside strong communication skills and fluency in Cantonese, English, and Putonghua. The position is distinctive in its focus on equitable access to AI literacy and 21st-century skills development, requiring applicants to analyze school-specific needs and adapt content for diverse audiences. Teaching experience, programming proficiency, and advanced knowledge of AI techniques are highly desirable, with the initial appointment made on a one-year contract basis.

title: Works Supervisor I/II (Builder Works) (carrying the job title of ‘Maintenance Officer’)
description: This position supervises building maintenance and renovation projects within the Estates Management Office, requiring the appointee to manage project works, lead internal staff in site inspections and maintenance activities, and perform regular technical building assessments, with flexibility for shift work and weekend availability as needed. Candidates must hold a Diploma or Higher Certificate in Building Studies (or equivalent qualification with 5+ years' relevant site experience) plus at least 3 years of supervisory experience in builder works and staff management, along with knowledge of facilities management software; a valid driving licence is advantageous. The role offers two entry levels—Works Supervisor I for those meeting all requirements, or Works Supervisor II for candidates with 1 year's post-qualification experience—with shortlisted applicants required to complete a written assessment. Initial appointment is on a 2-year renewable contract basis with gratuity upon contract completion.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: The position involves conducting translational research on age-related neurodegenerative diseases within an integrated neuroscience laboratory based at Prince of Wales Hospital, alongside laboratory maintenance responsibilities. Candidates must hold a Master's degree or Bachelor's degree in Biological Science or related fields with at least one year of relevant post-qualification experience, coupled with strong communication skills and self-motivation. Preferred experience includes expertise in molecular assays (Western blot, ELISA, qPCR), cell culture, histology, in vivo experimentation (MCAO, stereotactic injection, behavioral assays), or programming, though applicants with less experience may be considered for junior-level positions. The appointment is offered on an initial one-year contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Project Assistant II
department: Minor Programme in Entrepreneurship and Innovation
description: This Project Assistant II role supports an interdisciplinary entrepreneurship and innovation programme by managing social media and website content, coordinating administrative and logistical tasks for courses and events, liaising with internal and external partners, and providing clerical support. Candidates must have completed secondary education (preferably with a Bachelor's degree), demonstrate proficiency in English and Chinese at specified levels, possess strong computer skills including MS Office and ideally graphic design tools, and exhibit excellent communication and organizational abilities. The position offers a distinctive opportunity to contribute to fostering entrepreneurial culture and student engagement across campus through diverse programme operations. The appointment is initially for two years on a contract basis with renewal possibilities, and fresh graduates are encouraged to apply.

title: Executive Officer II [carrying the job title of 'Visitor Centre Development Specialist' (Project‑Based)]
description: This project-based role leads the complete redevelopment and rebranding of a visitor centre for the Communications and Public Relations Office, encompassing concept design, construction, operations, and visitor experience delivery. The appointee will oversee the full lifecycle from planning through sustainable operation, managing consultants and contractors, developing a viable business model with diversified revenue streams, and ensuring exceptional user experience through thoughtful design and service excellence. The position requires a Bachelor's degree in Business, eCommerce, Marketing, Interior Design, or related field, plus at least 10 years of demonstrated experience in retail operations, product development, or brand management, with proven success in managing renovation or new retail setup projects. The ideal candidate will possess strong expertise in brand strategy, customer experience design, retail environment setup, and the ability to manage complex operations within large organisational structures.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
description: This position coordinates undergraduate admissions across a large science faculty with over 4,000 students, encompassing application processing, interview arrangement, document validation, offer management, and applicant engagement. The role also involves supporting admissions events, creating promotional materials and social media content, managing admission scholarships, and assisting newly admitted students. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree and have at least two years of administrative experience, ideally in higher education, with strong proficiency in Microsoft 365, Chinese and English languages (including Cantonese and Putonghua), and demonstrated organizational and interpersonal skills. The position is offered on a renewable two-year contract basis, with shortlisted candidates required to complete a written test.

title: Scientific Officer
description: This Scientific Officer position in the Department of Surgery involves conducting research on innovative RIBOTAC and PROTAC therapeutics, managing laboratory operations, and supervising research staff and students while preparing publications and grant proposals. Candidates must hold a PhD in Biomedical or Analytical Chemistry with at least 2 years of postdoctoral research experience, strong knowledge of Molecular Biology and Analytical Chemistry, and a solid publication record, with laboratory management experience being advantageous. The role is initially offered on a 2-year renewable contract basis with performance-dependent continuation. Applications must be submitted online through the institutional career portal.

title: Clerical Assistant
department: Department of Anatomical and Cellular Pathology
description: This clerical assistant position supports the University Pathology Service at a hospital laboratory, handling daily administrative operations including document management, data entry, specimen tracking, and report processing. The role requires secondary education completion and strong bilingual (English/Chinese) skills in word processing, MS Office applications, and verbal communication. Key attributes include meticulous attention to detail, responsibility, self-motivation, teamwork ability, and capacity to work independently, with duties also encompassing specimen and report delivery within and outside the hospital. The appointment is initially for one year and renewable based on performance.

title: Accountant II (carrying the job title of ‘Assistant Finance Manager’)
description: This position oversees financial controls, budgets, and regulatory compliance for various project accounts including donations and grants while supervising daily accounting operations and coordinating financial reporting and analysis. The successful candidate must hold a bachelor's degree and professional accounting qualification (HKICPA, ACCA, CA or equivalent) with at least four years of post-qualification experience in auditing and accounting, including full-set bookkeeping experience. Key competencies include strong analytical and organizational skills, proficiency in MS Office, fluency in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua), and the ability to work independently under tight deadlines, with SAP and PeopleSoft experience being advantageous. The role is offered on an initial two-year contract with gratuity and potential renewal based on performance.

title: Executive Officer II (Innovation and Impact Development)
description: This role supports the Knowledge Transfer Office by managing strategic initiatives, major funding programmes, and administrative operations. Key responsibilities include stakeholder liaison and negotiation, end-to-end funding programme management (planning, tracking, compliance, reporting), high-level secretarial support, drafting reports and proposals, and coordinating events and outreach activities. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree or above with at least 2 years of post-qualification executive experience, ideally involving stakeholder negotiation and grant management, alongside fluent written and spoken English and Chinese, strong organisational and analytical skills, and excellent interpersonal and communication abilities. The position is offered initially on a 2-year contract basis with potential renewal, and shortlisted candidates will be required to sit a written test.

title: Part-time Project Assistant II
department: Centre for Health Education and Health Promotion
description: This part-time Project Assistant role supports health promotion initiatives within a centre dedicated to coordinating health education programmes across disciplines. Primary responsibilities include conducting workshops and health activities in schools and NGOs, managing collected data through entry and analysis, preparing workshop materials, and providing administrative support to various health promotion projects. Candidates must have completed secondary education with specified grades in at least five subjects including Chinese and English, alongside strong bilingual proficiency, excellent communication skills, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively; prior experience in health-related fields such as health promotion, nutrition, or public health is advantageous. The position is offered initially on a nine-month renewable contract, requiring submission of a curriculum vitae and relevant educational certificates through the online application system.

title: Project Assistant II (carrying the job title of ‘Assistant Programme Officer’)
description: This position supports graduate programme administration, focusing on admissions, registration, student data processing, and general postgraduate office duties. Candidates must have secondary education completion with specified passes in Chinese and English, at least two years of administrative experience (preferably in higher education), strong typing and computer skills, and fluency in English, Chinese, and Putonghua. The role requires meticulous attention to detail, analytical ability, and the capacity to manage tight deadlines, with shortlisted candidates required to complete a written assessment. The appointment is initially contract-based for up to two years with potential renewal based on performance.

title: Part-time Nurse
description: This part-time nursing role involves supporting research operations through administrative coordination, data collection and management, literature review, and manuscript preparation. Candidates must be registered nurses in Hong Kong with a valid Practising Certificate and at least three years of post-registration clinical experience, along with fluent bilingual communication skills (English and Cantonese) and strong computer proficiency. The position requires the ability to work independently, multitask effectively, and demonstrate high responsibility and self-motivation. The appointment is contract-based for up to two months, with applicants required to submit a CV and academic credentials demonstrating their professional qualifications.

title: Project Assistant II (carrying the job title of ‘Assistant Programme Administrator’)
description: The role involves providing administrative and clerical support to postgraduate nursing programs, including assisting students with admissions enquiries, coordinating program events, and handling general administrative duties. Applicants must have completed secondary education with strong qualifications (Grade E or equivalent in at least 5 subjects including Chinese and English), at least 2 years of relevant program administration experience, and proficiency in word processing and standard computer applications. The position requires strong written and spoken English and Chinese language skills. This is a 2-year fixed contract position with gratuity, renewable based on performance.

title: Part-time Project Co-ordinator II (carrying the job title of 'Promotion Officer')
