description: This part-time position coordinates a research project on end-of-life care for people with intellectual disabilities, requiring the incumbent to manage clerical and data support, organize promotional activities, create social media content, and assist with video production. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree, demonstrate fluency in English and Chinese, and have at least three years of post-qualification experience, ideally in marketing or event organization. The role demands strong communication, interpersonal, and time management skills, with the ability to work independently and manage expectations across internal and external stakeholders. The appointment is initially for one year and renewable based on performance.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This position offers research support for a project investigating Generative AI applications in analyzing and regenerating Lingnan water-adaptive architectural heritage through multi-scalar morphological analysis. The successful candidate should hold a Master's degree in Urban Design, Architecture, or Data Science, with strong proficiency in GIS and Adobe Suite, and ideally experience with Generative AI tools like Stable Diffusion and machine learning. Key responsibilities include conducting morphological analysis of heritage sites, developing specialized image datasets with performance-based captioning, fine-tuning AI models, coordinating workshops, and preparing academic publications for international venues. The role is initially a three-month renewable contract with potential pathways to PhD study and competitive fellowship funding for outstanding candidates.

title: Laboratory Assistant
description: This Laboratory Assistant position in the nursing school involves preparing clinical skills equipment, maintaining the simulation centre, supporting school events, and performing administrative tasks like record-keeping and data entry. Candidates must have completed secondary education with specific qualifications in Chinese and English plus three additional subjects, basic computer proficiency, strong interpersonal skills, and the ability to work independently; prior experience with nursing equipment is advantageous. The role is distinctive for combining technical laboratory support with administrative responsibilities in a clinical education setting. Initial appointment is for one year, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Educational Administration and Policy
description: The position is a Research Assistant supporting a project investigating school networks and system leadership across selected schools in the Greater Bay Area. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree or higher in Education or Social Sciences with at least one year of research experience in educational contexts, along with fluent English and Chinese language skills and proficiency in survey design, interviews, and data analysis. Key responsibilities include conducting literature reviews, collecting and analyzing data, writing reports, and supporting the Project Leader as needed. The role is offered initially on a one-year contract with potential renewal, and applications are reviewed on a rolling basis until March 31, 2026.

title: Full-time/Part-time Research Assistant
description: This position offers a full-time or part-time research role supporting a project on privacy law and data protection reforms in Hong Kong. The Research Assistant will conduct literature reviews, assist with data collection and stakeholder interviews, analyze research findings, and help prepare outputs and workshop events. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Law or related field, demonstrate strong legal research and writing skills, and be fluent in English and Cantonese with subject-matter interest. The initial contract runs for at least six months with potential renewal based on performance and funding availability.

title: Research Assistant
description: The position is a Research Assistant supporting a digital humanities initiative combining generative AI with Chinese calligraphy, utilizing a large-scale dataset of historical handwritten documents from the late Qing to early Republican period. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, AI, or related technical fields with at least two years of post-qualification experience, particularly in AI or creative industries, alongside expertise in generative AI, computer vision, and OCR technologies. Key responsibilities include developing AI models for document analysis and calligraphy generation, conducting user studies, applying computer vision techniques to dataset processing, managing stakeholder coordination, and adapting research outputs for academic and educational audiences. The role offers distinctive opportunities to contribute to AI advancement while directly impacting museum exhibitions and secondary school curricula, requiring strong technical foundations combined with the ability to communicate complex concepts across technical and non-technical audiences.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position in pediatric leukemia genomics seeks researchers to investigate genetic predisposition and oncogenic mechanisms in childhood acute lymphoblastic leukemia, with particular focus on germline GATA3 variants and CRLF2-rearranged B-cell leukemia. Candidates must hold a PhD in genetics, molecular biology, bioinformatics, or related fields (obtained within five years), demonstrate strong publication records in cancer or functional genomics, and possess proficiency in R/Python and genomic analysis pipelines, with preferred experience in molecular biology or genome engineering. Primary responsibilities include conducting independent and collaborative research on germline and somatic drivers of leukemia, analyzing large-scale genomic datasets with emphasis on non-coding functional annotation, performing CRISPR-Cas9 editing and molecular biology experiments, publishing findings, and mentoring graduate students. The appointment is a two-year renewable contract under the

title: Research Associate(s) / Senior Research Assistant(s) (External Fund)
description: The Department of Psychiatry seeks Research Associate(s) or Senior Research Assistant(s) to support a large-scale cohort and clinical trial investigating the relationship between sleep, circadian disturbances, and mental health in adolescents across a two-phase study design. Candidates must hold a Master's degree (Research Associates) or Bachelor's degree (Senior Research Assistants) in Social Sciences, Public Health, Sciences, or related fields, plus at least four years of relevant post-qualification experience, alongside proficiency in English and Cantonese, strong organisational skills, and demonstrated research enthusiasm. Key responsibilities include overseeing project implementation and timelines, recruiting participants and liaising with local schools, supervising junior staff, executing study protocols, conducting data analysis, and report writing. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract basis, with preference given to candidates with youth mental health research experience and statistical analysis knowledge.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in Finance seeks a recent graduate with strong quantitative and coding abilities to support research projects spanning environmental, development, and macroeconomic topics under the supervision of faculty at two institutions. Essential qualifications include a Bachelor's degree, proficiency in programming (particularly R), familiarity with AI tools, advanced statistics or econometrics knowledge, and clear English communication skills, with no prior finance background required. The role involves technical work such as writing R code for data visualization, automating PDF information extraction via APIs, and improving Julia code for equilibrium solvers, making it ideal for candidates interested in pursuing a PhD in Economics or Finance. The appointment is initially contract-based for one year and requires applicants to submit a CV, transcript, writing sample, code sample, and referee contacts online.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
department: Department of Otorhinolaryngology, Head and Neck Surgery
description: This research assistant position supports AI-driven research projects in otolaryngology, focusing on diagnostics, rhinology, skull base surgery, and olfactory sciences. The successful candidate will hold a bachelor's degree in a relevant field with strong competency in data analysis, programming (Python or R), and AI healthcare applications, alongside excellent analytical and communication skills. Primary responsibilities include data collection and analysis, collaborating with clinical teams to implement AI innovations, contributing to publications, and providing administrative and basic laboratory support. The role offers a one-year renewable contract providing hands-on experience in a multidisciplinary research environment.

title: Junior Research Assistant
description: Junior Research Assistant positions are available in Orthopaedics and Traumatology, based at a hospital in Hong Kong. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Biomedical Sciences, Pharmacology, or related fields, with demonstrated research experience or clinical research interest, and fluency in both Cantonese and English. The role involves supporting team-based research projects, with project management or clinical research experience being advantageous. Appointments are made on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance.

title: Executive Officer I (Innovation and Impact Development)
description: This position leads innovation and impact development initiatives within the Knowledge Transfer Office, managing stakeholder engagement, major competitive funding programmes (RAISe+, TSSSU, government innovation funds), and strategic reporting while providing secretarial support to senior committees. Candidates must hold at least a Bachelor's degree and demonstrate 8+ years of executive experience with a proven track record in securing and managing substantial competitive grants, coupled with expertise in drafting funding applications and policy documents. Essential qualifications include fluency in written and spoken English and Chinese, exceptional strategic and analytical abilities, strong negotiation skills for senior-level liaison, and proficiency in managing multiple complex projects autonomously under tight deadlines. The role is distinctive for its focus on bridging internal university units with external partners to advance research impact and innovation, requiring both high-level administrative competency and deep knowledge of higher education funding landscapes.

title: Research Associate (6-month contract)
department: Office for Greater Bay Area Developments
description: This six-month contract position supports a university office coordinating Greater Bay Area development initiatives, with a focus on organizing a national marine knowledge competition. The Research Associate will manage project execution, coordinate with mainland and Hong Kong stakeholders, assist committees, and provide logistical support for events and daily operations. Candidates must hold a Master's degree with at least four years of relevant experience, demonstrate fluency in English, Cantonese, and Putonghua, and possess strong project coordination skills—preferably with cross-border government collaborations. The role emphasizes interpersonal communication, organizational ability, and proficiency in standard office software.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
description: The Project Co-ordinator II role supports a newly established research centre focused on Electronic Design Automation, requiring comprehensive administrative oversight across finance, personnel management, committee secretarial duties, and research project coordination. The successful candidate must hold a bachelor's degree with at least two years of post-qualification project management experience, ideally in higher education, alongside fluency in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua) and strong proficiency in computer applications. Key responsibilities include managing research assistants, organizing workshops and large-scale events, handling budget and expense administration, and providing executive support across multiple operational areas. The position is offered on an initial two-year contract with renewal contingent on performance, and candidates with solid conference organization experience are particularly valued.

title: Project Co-ordinator II (Manuscript Editing)
department: The Chinese University of Hong Kong Press
description: This role involves overseeing the complete publication lifecycle of scholarly manuscripts in a bilingual English-Chinese academic press, from submission through final proofing, while coordinating with authors, contributors, and external vendors. The position requires a Bachelor's degree in Humanities or Social Sciences, at least three years of editorial experience, and demonstrated proficiency in both written and spoken English and Chinese, along with expertise in Chicago Manual of Style and traditional Chinese materials editing. Key responsibilities include managing project communications, handling administrative tasks such as database management and budget review, and maintaining detailed project documentation. The appointment is offered initially on a one-year renewable contract based on performance.

title: Executive Officer I/II (carrying the job title of ‘Senior Manager / Manager’), Office of Taught Postgraduate Programmes
description: This position in the Faculty of Business Administration's Office of Taught Postgraduate Programmes seeks an experienced administrator to manage admissions and student-related operations for taught postgraduate programmes. The successful candidate will supervise a team of executives, compile admissions data, coordinate student recruitment activities, and liaise with internal and external stakeholders while ensuring operational efficiency and high-quality outcomes. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree, meet specified English language requirements, and possess at least 8 years of post-qualification executive experience, ideally in higher education, with strong expertise in data analytics, presentation skills, and stakeholder management. The role requires flexibility including weekend work and business travel, and applicants with strong problem-solving abilities and change management experience are particularly valued.

title: Assistant Computer Officer
department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
description: This role involves establishing and maintaining the department's network infrastructure, Linux servers, and IT systems while developing web applications and providing technical support to staff and students. The ideal candidate holds a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related field with demonstrated experience in network administration, Linux server management, and web programming, alongside strong problem-solving abilities. Additional expertise in VPN setup, high-performance computing, or Cisco certifications (CCNA/CCNP) would be advantageous. The position is offered initially on a 2-year renewable contract with performance-based continuation.

title: Executive Officer II
description: This Executive Officer II position in the Nursing School requires providing executive and administrative support to the Graduate Division, managing postgraduate programmes, coordinating academic activities, handling student matters, and supervising staff. The successful candidate must hold a Bachelor's degree, demonstrate at least 5 years of executive experience in academic administration (preferably at tertiary institutions), and possess fluent English and Chinese proficiency including Cantonese and Putonghua. Key requirements include strong organisational and interpersonal skills, the ability to work independently under pressure, and proficiency with computers, with experience in curriculum management and student affairs being advantageous. The position is offered on an initial 2-year renewable contract, with shortlisted candidates required to sit a written test and applicants needing to submit academic certificates as proof of qualifications.

title: Transport Assistant II(s) (carrying the job title of ‘Assistant Transport Operations Officer’)
description: This position involves managing campus transportation operations, including overseeing shuttle services, coordinating bus schedules and maintenance, handling school bus services, and providing administrative support. Candidates must have secondary education completion, HKCEE/HKDSE qualifications (Grade E or Level 2+ in at least 5 subjects including Chinese and English), two years of clerical/administrative experience preferably in tertiary or transportation sectors, and valid licenses for private cars and light goods vehicles. Essential requirements include strong English and Chinese communication skills, typing proficiency, computer literacy (MS Office), knowledge of vehicle maintenance, responsibility, and teamwork. The role is a 2-year renewable contract position requiring 5-day weekly attendance and occasional outdoor inspections and overtime work.

title: Project Co-ordinator II(s)
department: The Hong Kong Jockey Club Sports Medicine and Health Sciences Centre
description: The position involves coordinating and executing community sports medicine events, providing secretarial and administrative support to sports medicine associations, and assisting with postgraduate programmes and short courses at a hospital-based centre. Candidates require a bachelor's degree and at least three years of experience in event/project management or administrative support, alongside strong problem-solving, interpersonal, and bilingual communication skills in English and Chinese. The role demands self-initiative, the ability to work independently and multitask, and occasional work beyond standard office hours. Appointments are offered initially on a one-year renewable contract.

title: Executive Officer II (carrying the job title of 'Manager, Content and Event'), Alumni Affairs and Development Office
description: The role is a Manager of Content and Events within the Alumni Affairs and Development Office, responsible for creating and managing all content and event-related activities for a prominent business school's extensive alumni network of over 47,000 members. Key responsibilities include producing bilingual content (English and Chinese) across multiple formats, managing digital communication channels and marketing platforms, organizing alumni events with full logistical oversight, supervising freelance contributors, and tracking campaign effectiveness. The position requires a bachelor's degree (preferably in communication, public relations, advertising, marketing, or related fields), strong bilingual writing and design capabilities, project management skills, and flexibility to work outside standard hours and travel as needed. This is a strategic role that bridges content creation, stakeholder engagement, and event execution to support the school's alumni community and fundraising objectives.

title: Clerical Assistant(s) (carrying the job title of ‘Operations Assistant’)
description: This Operations Assistant position in the Transport Office combines clerical administrative work with customer-facing and operational duties. Responsibilities include standard office tasks (filing, data entry), counter service with passenger enquiry and complaint handling, management of campus school bus services and facilities, and occasional driving duties as needed. Candidates must have completed secondary education, strong word processing and computer skills, a valid driving licence for cars and light goods vehicles, fluent written and spoken English and Chinese, and excellent interpersonal and customer service abilities. The role is offered on a two-year renewable contract, with preference given to applicants with prior experience in customer service or transportation sectors.

title: Executive Officer II (carrying the job title of 'Manager, CRM and Digital Engagement'), Alumni Affairs and Development Office
description: The position is a Manager of CRM and Digital Engagement within the Alumni Affairs and Development Office, responsible for managing customer relationship management systems and platforms to support alumni engagement, event management, and fundraising initiatives. Key responsibilities include overseeing CRM tools and digital channels, maintaining data integrity through cleansing and segmentation, generating performance reports, managing events across online and offline formats, and collaborating with internal teams and external vendors on system improvements. The role requires strong technical proficiency with CRM systems, data management expertise, project management skills, and the ability to support strategic alumni engagement initiatives. This position is distinctive in its focus on leveraging technology and data to drive alumni community cultivation for a large international business school with over 47,000 alumni worldwide.

title: Assistant Librarian II (carrying the job title of 'Special Collections Librarian')
department: The Chinese University of Hong Kong Library
description: This position seeks a Special Collections Librarian to manage and develop an extensive collection of rare and unique materials focused on Chinese and Hong Kong studies, ranging from ancient oracle bones to contemporary literary archives. The role involves acquiring materials through donor relations, liaising with faculty, supporting outreach and exhibition development, and providing professional guidance on collection management and preservation. Candidates must hold a postgraduate qualification in librarianship or information science with at least three years of relevant professional experience, along with expertise in archival standards (DACS, EAD, MARC) and systems like ArchivesSpace, plus demonstrated supervisory and collaborative abilities. Fluency in both Chinese and English is desirable, and an advanced degree in China Studies or related disciplines is advantageous; the appointment is initially for two years on a contract basis.

title: Assistant Librarian II (carrying the job title of ‘Digital Transformation Services Librarian’)
department: The Chinese University of Hong Kong Library
description: This position is a Digital Transformation Services Librarian reporting to the Head of Digital Initiatives, responsible for leading the university library's digital transformation strategy through developing and implementing emerging technologies, managing complex digital projects, and delivering innovative research services. Key responsibilities include spearheading digital service improvements, building technical capacity for digitization and digital repositories, developing web and software applications across their full lifecycle, and fostering collaboration with internal and external stakeholders on technical standards and best practices. The ideal candidate holds a university degree in computer science or software engineering combined with a postgraduate qualification in librarianship, has at least three years of professional experience, and demonstrates proficiency in Web application development, Python programming, and familiarity with emerging technologies such as text-mining and computer vision. The role requires flexibility to perform evening and weekend helpdesk duties, alongside proven expertise in leading digital transformation and change management initiatives.

title: Project Co-ordinator II(s) (carrying the job title of ‘Executive, Corporate Communications’)
description: The Communications and Public Relations Office seeks Project Co-ordinator II professionals to support strategic communication objectives through press event planning, media relations, content creation, and issue management. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree (preferably in Communications or Journalism) and have at least 5 years of experience in public relations or corporate communications, ideally within large corporations, agencies, or media organisations. Essential qualifications include fluency in written and spoken English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua), strong organisational and interpersonal skills, and proven analytical and time management abilities. The position is offered on an initial 2-year renewable contract, with shortlisted applicants required to complete written tests in English and Chinese.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant (The Hong Kong Genome Project)
description: This position supports a government-backed genomic medicine initiative, requiring a Bachelor's degree in genetics, health sciences, biomedical science, or related fields, with at least two years of relevant experience. The role involves conducting basic research, managing project correspondence, assisting genetic counsellors, recruiting and interviewing study participants in clinical settings, and handling administrative duties. Candidates should be detail-oriented team players with strong English and Chinese language skills, though experience in patient care, clinical research, or digital health technologies is advantageous. The appointment is initially contract-based for one year, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Associate (carrying the job title of “Project Manager”)
description: The Department of Obstetrics and Gynaecology seeks a Research Associate/Project Manager to establish and manage high-quality prospective clinical studies. Candidates must hold a Master's degree in Biology, Biochemistry, Life Science or related field, with at least four years of clinical research experience, fluency in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua), and strong interpersonal skills. Key responsibilities include developing management systems, securing regulatory approvals, coordinating study documentation, training research sites, managing budgets, monitoring protocol compliance, supervising data collection and validation, and reporting progress to oversight committees and funders. The position is offered initially on a one-year fixed contract, renewable based on performance.

title: Assistant Director of Human Resources (at the rank of Senior Assistant Secretary II)
description: This position is an Assistant Director of Human Resources responsible for overseeing recruitment, staff engagement, performance management, benefits, and learning and development within the university's HR team, with a particular focus on leading talent development strategies, performance management enhancement, and succession planning. The role requires strong leadership capability to manage team members across various levels and support academic and administrative units in workforce planning, staffing decisions, and regulatory compliance. Ideal candidates should possess a strong academic background with fluent English and Chinese (including Putonghua), substantial senior-level HR or public administration experience preferably in higher education, comprehensive employment law knowledge, and excellent communication and analytical abilities. The position is offered on a three-year renewable contract basis, with shortlisted candidates required to complete a written test.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Anatomical and Cellular Pathology
description: This postdoctoral position focuses on lung cancer research, including genomics, tumor microenvironment investigation, and therapeutic development. Candidates must hold a Ph.D. in Biological or Chemical Sciences with molecular biology experience, or a Ph.D. in Computer Science with bioinformatics expertise, along with demonstrated commitment to research. The fellow will conduct independent research, publish findings, apply for grants, and mentor junior researchers at a hospital research facility. The initial one-year contract is renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Part-time Research Assistant
description: This part-time research assistant position requires a Bachelor's degree in a health-related field (preferably Nursing) plus two to three years of relevant work experience, with demonstrated capability in conducting and coordinating research studies using both qualitative and quantitative methods. Key responsibilities include assisting with data collection, implementing intervention protocols, performing literature reviews, managing data, and writing reports, while maintaining proficiency in English and Chinese and competence with MS Office and SPSS. The role demands independent work ability and offers a one-year renewable contract commencing immediately, with candidates required to submit their CV, qualification certificates, and supporting documents through the online application portal.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
department: Department of Anatomical and Cellular Pathology
description: This postdoctoral or research associate position supports breast cancer research, requiring either a PhD or a Master's degree with five years of cancer research experience. The successful candidate will conduct experimental work in cell biology, animal studies, and molecular techniques while collaborating with postgraduate students and supporting the writing of manuscripts and grant proposals at a hospital-based research facility. Key competencies include solid technical expertise in molecular biology, strong research motivation, and data management skills, with next-generation sequencing and bioinformatics experience being advantageous. The initial appointment is for one year on a contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
department: Department of Anatomical and Cellular Pathology
description: This position is a Research Assistant or Junior Research Assistant in pathology conducting cancer research across multiple sites. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree or higher in Biomedical Sciences or related fields, with practical experience in animal handling, tissue culture, and molecular analytical techniques. The role involves collaborative research with department staff and offers an initial one-year contract renewable by mutual agreement, making it suitable for recent graduates seeking postgraduate experience. Required application materials include a CV detailing academic qualifications and research experience, academic certificates, and two reference letters.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant (carrying the job title of ‘Project Officer/Assistant Project Officer’)
description: This Research Assistant position in Electronic Engineering requires a Bachelor's degree and preferably two years of tertiary experience, with strong bilingual English-Chinese communication skills and proficiency in MS Office and graphic design. The role involves assisting with program administration, project management, and supporting the organization of postgraduate events and activities. Applicants must demonstrate responsibility, self-motivation, and independent working capability; fresh graduates are eligible. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract, with applications submitted online via cover letter and CV.

title: Assistant Human Resources Officer
description: This position involves supporting human resources administration, including personnel services, staff leave, benefits management, and general office operations while handling correspondence and staff enquiries. Candidates must have completed secondary education with specified qualifications in Chinese and English, alongside strong word-processing, IT proficiency (MS Office), and bilingual communication skills; prior human resources or administrative experience is valued but not essential. The role is initially offered on a two-year contract basis with potential renewal and opportunities for advancement to Human Resources Officer for degree-qualified candidates demonstrating strong performance. Applicants must submit certified copies of academic and language qualification documents and will be required to complete a written assessment and IT assignment if shortlisted for interview.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: The Department of Psychiatry seeks Research Assistants/Junior Research Assistants to support a large-scale cohort and clinical trial investigating the relationship between sleep, circadian disturbances, and mental health in adolescents. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree (preferably in Social Sciences, Public Health, or related disciplines), be fluent in English and Cantonese, and demonstrate strong organizational skills and motivation; prior research experience in youth mental health or statistical knowledge is preferred. Primary responsibilities include conducting clinical and research interviews, performing neurocognitive and sleep assessments, assisting with psychological intervention development, and managing data entry and analysis, with on-the-job training provided. Positions are offered initially on a one-year contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) – Cross Cultural Analytics (Data Science, NLP, Computational Linguistics, Computational Humanities)
description: Two full-time postdoctoral fellows are sought to develop and apply NLP, data science, and AI methods to analyze large-scale historical texts examining Chinese cultural influence in Europe and North America from 1550–1900. Applicants must hold a PhD in computer science, NLP, computational linguistics, data science, or related technical fields, with strong Python programming skills and demonstrated experience processing textual data using modern NLP tools and machine learning frameworks. Key responsibilities include developing OCR correction and semantic search methods, constructing multilingual historical corpora, training graph neural networks, and co-authoring research papers for digital humanities and NLP venues in collaboration with an international team. Preference is given to candidates with multilingual NLP experience, background in digital text pipelines or graph neural networks, and interest in cultural analytics, though prior historical training is not required.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: This position seeks a Postdoctoral Fellow or Research Associate to conduct research on corporate social responsibility and sustainability communication, requiring either a PhD (Fellow) or Master's degree (Associate) in Communication or related fields, plus four years of relevant post-qualification experience. The successful candidate will support research activities across the full project lifecycle, including developing and implementing research protocols, managing data collection and analysis, and disseminating findings through publications and presentations, with particular value placed on quantitative, qualitative, and computational research capabilities. The role offers a one-year renewable contract and welcomes applications from candidates with interdisciplinary backgrounds in communication, business, management, marketing, organisational studies, or psychology who can demonstrate strong English communication skills and prior experience in CSR or sustainability-related research.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Assistant(s)
description: This position invites Postdoctoral Fellows and Research Assistants to support cutting-edge translational research integrating AI, augmented reality, and surgical navigation technologies in orthopaedics, with projects spanning AR-based surgical systems, AI-assisted planning tools, diagnostic platforms, and LLM-enabled surgical planning. Postdoctoral Fellows should hold a PhD in biomedical engineering, computer science, or related fields with proven expertise in grant writing, publications, and project management, while Research Assistants require a Bachelor's or Master's degree with strong interest in medical technologies and AI; experience with Python, medical imaging, or AR/VR is advantageous but not essential for either role. Key responsibilities include preparing grant proposals and manuscripts, coordinating with collaborators, supporting software development and clinical data management, and tracking project milestones and reporting. Appointments are initially contract-based for one year, renewable based on performance, requiring submission of

title: Technician
description: This technician position supports the Orthopaedic Learning Centre and Department at a hospital facility, with responsibilities encompassing cadaver training coordination, workshop setup, audio-visual and medical equipment management, website administration, and general departmental duties, with flexibility for evening and weekend work required. Candidates must hold a diploma or certificate in a technical field (ideally Biological Sciences) with at least three years of relevant experience, combined with strong multitasking abilities, bilingual proficiency in Chinese and English, and solid computer skills. The role demands independent and collaborative work capabilities, alongside excellent communication, customer service orientation, attention to detail, and self-motivation, with prior experience in audio-visual applications and medical instrumentation preferred. Initial appointment is offered on a one-year renewable contract contingent upon performance.

title: Assistant Officer (at the rank of General Clerk II)
description: The role is an administrative and secretarial position supporting the Finance Office Director, with responsibilities including office coordination, staff supervision, documentation management, stakeholder liaison, and general office operations. Candidates must have completed secondary education with specified language qualifications in Chinese and English, demonstrated proficiency in MS Office and Chinese/English word-processing, strong bilingual communication skills, and at least three years of full-time administrative or clerical experience, ideally at tertiary institutions. Supervisory experience managing front-line staff is preferred, and shortlisted candidates will complete a written test. The appointment is initially for two years on a contract basis with gratuity and potential renewal based on performance.

title: Assistant Computer Officer
description: The Assistant Computer Officer will support IT infrastructure and lab operations in the Department of Information Engineering, including designing and implementing coursework systems, providing technical support to staff and students, and offering occasional on-site support outside regular hours. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in a related field with at least 3 years of hands-on experience in system administration, network management, and user support, along with demonstrated proficiency in networking, Linux, Windows Server, security incident handling, and laboratory setup. The role requires strong troubleshooting, communication, and customer service skills, with the ability to work independently and manage multiple projects under pressure. Additional expertise in cybersecurity, virtualization, cloud computing, system automation, and emerging technologies such as GPU management and LLM applications is highly desirable.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
description: This Project Co-ordinator II position involves providing comprehensive administrative and secretarial support to a robotics research institute, including expense management, human resources coordination, website maintenance, and research project assistance. The successful candidate will need a Bachelor's degree, at least one year of project management experience, and strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (Putonghua), alongside excellent organizational, problem-solving, and interpersonal skills. The role requires someone who is detail-oriented, proactive, and capable of working independently and collaboratively in a dynamic research environment. The position is offered on an initial 2-year renewable contract, and prior experience in university event organization or accounting would be advantageous.

title: Executive Officer II
department: Hong Kong Institute of Educational Research
description: This role provides executive and administrative support to programme coordinators managing self-financed and in-service teacher education initiatives. Key responsibilities include managing all aspects of academic programme operations—from admissions and scheduling to budgeting, quality assurance, and student services—along with secretarial support for programme committees and assistance in curriculum development. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree with at least 5 years of post-qualification executive experience, demonstrate fluency in English and Chinese (both written and spoken), and possess advanced MS Office proficiency alongside strong organisational and communication skills. The position is offered on a 1-year renewable contract, with flexibility to work beyond regular office hours when needed.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: The Department of Surgery seeks Research Assistant(s) to support machine learning projects focused on diagnosing and treating cardio-thoracic conditions, particularly thoracic aortic aneurysms and dissections. Candidates must hold at least a Bachelor's degree in healthcare, biomedical engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, or related fields, with foundational knowledge of cardiovascular anatomy, medical imaging modalities, and demonstrated expertise in machine learning development. Key responsibilities include designing and optimizing machine learning systems to enhance diagnostic workflows and standardize reporting for these conditions, alongside research administrative duties. The position is offered on a renewable one-year contract basis, requiring strong programming skills, fluency in English, and excellent communication and organizational abilities.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in the Department of Finance seeks candidates with a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Actuarial Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, or related fields, along with strong programming proficiency in R, MATLAB, and/or Python. The role involves assisting with actuarial science research and performing assigned duties, with preference given to applicants with prior research experience in actuarial science or statistics; PhD students permitted to work full-time are also eligible. The position is offered initially on a six-month contract basis, renewable based on performance and mutual agreement, and applicants must submit a resume and description of relevant research and programming experience along with information about their availability and Hong Kong work eligibility.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in nursing requires a Bachelor's degree plus at least two years of relevant experience in nursing, public health, or related disciplines, with demonstrated expertise in quantitative and qualitative research, grant writing, and manuscript preparation. Key responsibilities include initiating and conducting research projects, analyzing data, drafting grant proposals and academic publications, and supporting the School Research Committee administratively. Proficiency in both English and Chinese (written and spoken) is essential. The role is offered on a one-year renewable contract basis and requires submission of a CV and academic certificates demonstrating language proficiency and qualifications.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant (carrying the job title of ‘Project Officer/Assistant Project Officer’)
department: Department of Mechanical and Automation Engineering
description: This position offers a research support role within a mechanical engineering department, carrying the title of Project Officer or Assistant Project Officer depending on experience level. Applicants require a bachelor's degree and preferably 2-3 years of tertiary institution experience, along with strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, excellent communication and organizational skills, and proficiency with standard office software and Chinese word processing. The role involves assisting with office administration, project management, and coordinating postgraduate programme events and activities. The appointment is offered on a two-year renewable contract basis, with prior postgraduate programme administration experience valued but not essential.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: A Research Assistant or Junior Research Assistant position in the Department of Psychiatry seeks support for sleep and mental health research studies. Candidates should hold a bachelor's degree or higher in psychology, counselling, medical sciences, social work or related fields, speak Cantonese fluently, and demonstrate strong organizational and communication skills alongside a genuine interest in mental and sleep health research. The role encompasses participant recruitment and communication, conducting clinical and research interviews, data entry and analysis, with occasional work outside standard office hours; relevant experience in psychological or psychiatric research is preferred. The appointment is initially contract-based for three months, renewable by mutual agreement, with on-the-job training provided.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Statistics and Data Science
description: This Research Assistant position is embedded within a world-leading equine doping control laboratory at a major racecourse, offering the opportunity to work on biomarker discovery and validation for equine sports integrity. The role requires a Bachelor's degree in Molecular Biology, Biochemistry, or related field, along with bilingual English-Chinese proficiency, strong organizational skills, and preferably hands-on experience with PCR, next-generation sequencing, and mass spectrometry techniques. Key responsibilities include conducting proteomic and transcriptomic analyses, working collaboratively with biochemists and bioinformaticians, performing laboratory maintenance and calibration, and ensuring compliance with international quality assurance standards. This position combines cutting-edge molecular research with practical laboratory work in a specialized, highly-regulated testing environment focused on equine sports and animal welfare.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
description: Postdoctoral fellows are sought to conduct nursing and health research, developing new projects, managing ongoing studies, and contributing to grant writing and academic publications. Candidates must hold a PhD in nursing, public health, health-related fields, or social sciences, with demonstrated expertise in quantitative or qualitative research, strong publication records, and fluency in both English and Chinese. The position offers an initial one-year contract, renewable based on performance and agreement. Applicants should apply online through the designated university careers portal.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: Applicants with a Bachelor's degree in Medical Laboratory Science, Biomedical Science, or related fields are sought for Research Assistant positions in the Department of Chemical Pathology, with laboratory experience and bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Mandarin) preferred. The role involves assisting with molecular diagnostics and circulating DNA research projects at two locations, including PCR and DNA sequencing work, molecular testing, data analysis, sample handling, and laboratory maintenance. Candidates must demonstrate self-motivation, responsibility, and strong teamwork skills, and the position is initially offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance. Academic certificates confirming qualifications are mandatory for application consideration, with online submission required through the designated career portal.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position is based at a hospital facility and supports public health research focused on behavioral and mental health promotion across Hong Kong and mainland China. The role involves coordinating research activities, leading group-based health promotion projects, and conducting data analysis, with on-the-job training provided in research methodology, biostatistics, and epidemiology. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in public health, biostatistics, epidemiology, psychology, or related fields, with statistical skills and fluency in English and Cantonese; prior research coordination or intervention project experience is advantageous. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Research Assistant(s)
description: The Sports Medicine Team seeks postdoctoral fellows, research associates, or research assistants to conduct basic research on musculoskeletal tissue repair, specifically investigating therapeutic effects and identifying novel markers in tendinopathy using transcriptomics and lipidomics approaches. Candidates should hold a PhD in relevant biological or biomedical disciplines with strong expertise in molecular and cell biology techniques, molecular characterisation methods, and a demonstrated publication record, though those with MPhil or BSc qualifications and relevant experience may be considered for associate or assistant roles. Responsibilities include experimental research on tissue repair, data analysis, preparing grant proposals and publications, and laboratory management duties. The position is initially offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement, with opportunity for PhD candidates to pursue concurrent doctoral studies under supervisor approval.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in the Department of Surgery requires a Master's degree in Biochemistry, Biological Sciences, or a related field, along with demonstrated expertise in mammalian cell culture and molecular techniques such as DNA/RNA extraction, RT-PCR, cytotoxicity assays, and immunofluorescence imaging. The role involves working directly with clinical specimens, including medical sample collection and processing of patient-derived organoids, making it distinctive for its clinical research focus. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement, commencing as soon as possible.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position supports quantitative marketing research projects, requiring candidates with a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Economics, Statistics, Business Analytics, or related fields, alongside proficiency in programming languages such as Excel, R, and Python. Key responsibilities include organizing and executing data collection, assisting with literature reviews and data organization, and providing administrative support for research operations under the supervision of Professor Hu. The role demands meticulous attention to detail, strong organizational skills, excellent English proficiency, and reliability in executing extended, detailed tasks, with prior experience as a Research Assistant in quantitative settings preferred. The position is initially offered on a six-month contract basis, renewable based on performance.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Mechanical and Automation Engineering
description: The position offers roles at multiple career levels (Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, or Research Assistant) within a project developing integrated unmanned aerial vehicle systems and digital traffic platforms, requiring expertise in robotics, artificial intelligence, and traffic modeling. Postdoctoral Fellows and Research Associates will conduct advanced research on UAV optimization, control algorithms, and traffic prediction, while Research Assistants will provide support through data collection, labeling, field testing, and model training. Candidates must hold a PhD (postdoctoral), Master's with four years of relevant experience (associate), or Bachelor's degree (assistant) in Computer Science, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Traffic Engineering, or related fields. The position is a two-year contract beginning March 2026, renewable based on performance, with applications required through the online careers portal including detailed CV and academic credentials.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Research Assistant(s) (External Fund)
department: The Jockey Club School of Public Health and Primary Care
description: The school is recruiting Postdoctoral Fellows, Research Associates, and Research Assistants to conduct health research using large administrative datasets. Candidates must hold a PhD in public health, epidemiology, biostatistics, or related fields (for postdoctoral roles), or a Master's degree with relevant experience, and demonstrate proficiency in statistical software and quantitative analysis. Responsibilities include developing research projects, managing datasets, writing proposals and publications, and assisting the Principal Investigator. Positions are initially contracted for one year with the possibility of renewal based on performance.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: Applicants with a Master's degree (Research Assistant) or Bachelor's/Diploma (Junior Research Assistant) in biological or medical sciences are sought to support microbiology research. Primary responsibilities include managing bacterial strains and stocks, ordering laboratory supplies, conducting laboratory tests, and performing experiments with associated data collection and reporting. The role offers hands-on experience in microbiology, biochemistry, and molecular biology, with work split between a hospital setting and a science park. The initial one-year contract position is renewable based on mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: The Jockey Club School of Public Health and Primary Care
description: The position involves conducting literature reviews, data extraction, quality assessments, and both qualitative and quantitative research analysis within a public health research environment. Candidates must hold a Master's degree (or Bachelor's for the Junior level) in Statistics, Public Health, Social Science, or related fields, with proficiency in statistical software (SPSS, STATA, SAS, R) and/or NVIVO, alongside fluent English and Chinese (Cantonese preferred). Key responsibilities include managing and analysing research data, preparing project reports, and performing related administrative duties, with experience in evidence-based medicine, guideline development, or traditional Chinese medicine being advantageous. The role is initially contracted for up to six months, renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral fellowship in finance requires a Ph.D. in Actuarial Science or related field, with demonstrated research excellence, strong English proficiency, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively. The fellow will conduct high-quality actuarial research, develop research proposals, write articles and reports, and present findings under faculty supervision. The one-year contract position begins in January 2026 and requires submission of a cover letter, CV with complete publication list, research proposal, two recent publications, and three referee contacts through the online application portal.

title: Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences
description: The Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences seeks Research Assistant(s) to support clinical and research programmes across multiple ophthalmic specialties, including ocular surface diseases, glaucoma, retinal diseases, and ocular cancers. Responsibilities include conducting ophthalmic investigations, extracting medical data, and coordinating patient follow-up schedules for research projects. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree, demonstrate proficiency in English and Cantonese, show genuine interest in medical research, and possess strong patient care skills; statistical analysis experience and clinical research background are advantageous. The role offers opportunities to contribute to landmark research publications, develop specialized ophthalmological skills, and pursue postgraduate studies, with an initial one-year renewable contract.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
department: School of Hotel and Tourism Management
description: The School of Hotel and Tourism Management seeks postdoctoral fellows with PhDs in real estate, finance, economics, or related fields to conduct independent and collaborative research in real estate economics, urban economics, household finance, and related areas. Candidates should possess strong programming skills (STATA, SAS, Python, R) and solid knowledge of econometrics and mathematical modeling, with PhD candidates at the dissertation stage also encouraged to apply. The position offers a one-year renewable contract beginning immediately or in August 2026, requiring fellows to produce publications suitable for top-tier journals and contribute to the school's research culture. Applicants must submit a cover letter, curriculum vitae with publication list, research statement, and three referee contacts through the online application portal.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Geography and Resource Management
description: This postdoctoral position involves conducting original, high-quality research under the supervision of Prof. Jack Harris in areas such as evolutionary economic geography, sustainable transitions, Green FinTech, and entrepreneurial ecosystems. Candidates must hold a PhD in Human Geography or related disciplines, demonstrate a strong publication record, possess excellent English proficiency, and show capability for both independent and collaborative work. The role is offered on an 18-month contract basis, with the start date flexible until March 1st, 2026. Applicants should submit a cover letter, CV, two-page research plan, and three referee contacts through the online application portal.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position in a pharmacy research team involves leading a Chinese medicine-funded project with responsibilities spanning research logistics, fieldwork oversight, data management, and mentoring of junior staff. Essential qualifications include a PhD in a healthcare or scientific discipline, strong statistical software proficiency, fluency in Cantonese and English, and proven independent research capability, with preference given to candidates with experience in artificial intelligence, project management, or implementation science. The role requires coordinating multi-site studies, developing educational materials, ensuring data quality across collection through analysis, and organizing training sessions. The initial one-year contract is renewable based on performance.

title: Research Associate
description: This Research Associate position supports a pharmaceutical research project within the School of Pharmacy, focusing on acquiring and processing global pharmaceutical data using web crawlers, deep learning, and large language models. The ideal candidate holds a doctoral degree in applied mathematics, engineering, statistics, pharmaceutical sciences, or related fields, with demonstrated programming expertise in languages such as Python or Java and preferably background knowledge in pharmacology, pharmacometrics, or systems pharmacology. Core responsibilities include data collection and classification, developing front-end and back-end interfaces, project monitoring, and independent problem-solving, requiring strong organizational and communication skills alongside proven research publication record. The appointment is contract-based for one year, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position supports a Chinese Medicine Development Fund project within the School of Pharmacy, requiring a Bachelor's degree in Pharmacy or related field and proficiency in reading pharmaceutical literature, office software, and data management tools. Key responsibilities include searching and synthesizing professional materials related to the project, organizing and classifying collected data, extracting critical information, standardizing pharmaceutical terminology for database specifications, and maintaining accurate records while supporting team collaboration. The ideal candidate should be meticulous, analytical, and capable of working independently or within a team under tight deadlines, with strong organizational and communication skills. The role is offered on a one-year renewable contract basis contingent on performance.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: This postdoctoral or research associate position in nursing requires either a PhD in nursing or a health-related field, or a Master's degree with four years of post-qualification research experience. The role focuses on initiating and developing research projects, conducting empirical studies, analyzing data, and supporting the preparation of grant proposals and academic publications. Candidates must demonstrate strong quantitative and/or qualitative research capabilities with an established publication record, along with fluency in written and spoken English and Chinese including Cantonese. The position is initially offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Associate / Research Assistant
description: This position offers research and administrative support to a top-tier finance department and its Finance for Society Research Centre, which bridges academic research with industry practice across areas including sustainable finance, behavioral finance, and fintech. Candidates should hold a PhD in Finance, Economics, or Business (or a Master's degree for Research Assistant level), with strong data analysis skills, bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, and demonstrated ability to work independently and collaboratively. Key responsibilities include supporting faculty research, organizing departmental and center activities, managing administrative functions, and coordinating with internal and external stakeholders. The role is a fixed two-year contract, renewable based on performance, reporting directly to the Department Chair.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: This postdoctoral or research associate position supports a major research centre dedicated to Chinese music and opera, which houses extensive archival collections and organises public engagement activities. The successful candidate will manage grant applications, coordinate conferences and events, engage with donors, support research office functions, and develop databases to track research impact, while collaborating closely with faculty members. Candidates must hold a PhD in Music, Chinese Studies, or related fields (or a Master's degree with four years of relevant experience), demonstrate fluency in English and Chinese, and possess proven expertise in grant writing, event management, archival work, and media engagement. The appointment is initially for two years on a contract basis, with potential renewal based on performance.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: The Research Assistant/Junior Research Assistant position supports the Preclinical PET/MR Imaging Laboratory at the neuroscience institute, with responsibilities encompassing facility operations, equipment maintenance and calibration, inventory management, and compliance coordination with campus safety and animal services. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's degree in Medical Imaging, Radiography, or related fields, possess strong organizational and communication skills, and be proficient in both English and Chinese, with prior laboratory research experience and animal handling experience preferred. The role emphasizes strict adherence to ethical standards in animal research and requires attention to detail in record-keeping and procurement. The position is initially offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Mechanical and Automation Engineering
description: This Research Assistant position supports cutting-edge research in learning-based control for surgical robotics, focusing on developing autonomous robotic systems that can perform complex surgical procedures through imitation and reinforcement learning. The role requires a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Robotics, or related fields, with essential hands-on experience in robotics simulators (such as Isaac-Sim, Mujoco, or Gazebo) and deep learning frameworks like PyTorch, alongside solid understanding of imitation and reinforcement learning theory. Key responsibilities include building realistic surgical simulation platforms, implementing and evaluating state-of-the-art learning algorithms, bridging simulation-to-reality transfer, and conducting real-world experiments with robotic hardware. The position offers a distinctive opportunity to work at the intersection of robotics, artificial intelligence, and medicine, with preference given to candidates with prior experience in ROS/ROS2, multi-robot control, and embodied

title: Full-time/Part-time Research Assistant
description: This position is a full-time or part-time research assistant role supporting research initiatives focused on preventive medicine and healthy lifestyle promotion. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's degree in a relevant field such as Information Technology, Health, or Social Science, with at least two years of research-related experience, ideally in research administration at a tertiary institution. Key responsibilities include providing administrative support for research matters, assisting with grant applications, organizing research activities, and liaising between academic departments and external partners. Essential qualifications include bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, strong IT skills, excellent coordination abilities, and the capacity to work independently with minimal supervision.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: Research assistants are sought to support health-risk behaviour research projects, requiring a bachelor's degree or higher in disciplines such as statistics, psychology, social sciences, or public health, along with fluency in English, Chinese, and Cantonese, strong computer literacy, and excellent organisational and communication skills. Primary responsibilities include implementing interventions, recruiting participants and collecting data, conducting literature reviews, managing data entry, and preparing reports and publications. Candidates must demonstrate self-motivation, responsibility, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively. Positions are offered on an initial one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences
description: The Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences seeks motivated Research Assistants or Junior Research Assistants to support clinical and translational research programmes spanning diverse eye diseases including glaucoma, retinal conditions, ocular cancers, and autoimmune disorders. Core responsibilities include conducting ophthalmic investigations, extracting and managing medical data, and coordinating participant follow-up schedules. Applicants require a Bachelor's degree (or equivalent experience for Junior positions), proficiency in English and Cantonese, strong organizational skills, and genuine interest in ophthalmology research; prior research experience and statistical analysis skills are advantageous. The role offers opportunities to contribute to landmark publications, develop specialized investigative expertise, and access career advancement and postgraduate study pathways, with initial appointment on a one-year renewable contract.

title: Research Assistant
department: Institute for Tissue Engineering and Regenerative Medicine
description: This Research Assistant position involves conducting laboratory research in tissue engineering and regenerative medicine under supervisory guidance, with responsibilities including primary cell culture, histology, immunohistochemistry, data analysis, and assisting personnel with technical skill development. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's degree in Biomedical Sciences or related field (Master's preferred) and possess practical experience in cell culture and histological techniques, along with strong organizational and communication skills in English and Chinese. The role emphasizes self-motivation, independent work capacity, and willingness to learn, with on-the-job training provided to develop expertise in the field. The position is offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: This postdoctoral position in the School of Biomedical Sciences seeks a PhD-holder with expertise in stem cell engineering, sequencing/gene editing technology, or regenerative biology for nerve injury research. The successful candidate will support the Principal Investigator in project management, establish and maintain research platforms, supervise junior team members, and contribute to manuscript and grant preparation. Applicants must demonstrate strong publication records, bilingual proficiency in Chinese and English, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively under pressure. The initial one-year contract is renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: This Research Assistant position in the Department of Orthopaedics and Traumatology offers an opportunity to advance AI applications in medical research, with primary focus on optimizing large language models using frameworks like PyTorch and TensorFlow, alongside Python and R programming. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Biomedical Science, Computer Science, or related fields, with demonstrated expertise in unsupervised deep learning, statistical programming in R, Python proficiency, and bilingual fluency in English and Chinese. Key responsibilities include conducting clinical trials, analyzing data, manipulating medical images, and performing assigned research and technical activities. The role is offered on a one-year renewable contract basis, commencing immediately.

title: Part-time Research Assistant
description: This part-time Research Assistant position in the School of Pharmacy supports Prof. Joyce You's research team, primarily conducting health economics research involving retrospective data collection and analysis of medical costs, literature reviews, and decision-analytic modeling. Candidates must hold at least a Bachelor's degree in Health Economics or related fields (Master's preferred) and demonstrate bilingual proficiency in Chinese and English alongside strong communication skills. The role encompasses database management, Delphi surveys, and various research support tasks assigned by the principal investigator. Applications require submission of a CV and relevant certificates through the online application portal.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This position seeks a Research Assistant or Junior Research Assistant in the School of Biomedical Sciences to conduct research on nerve injury and repair projects while coordinating laboratory operations. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Medical or Biomedical Sciences and demonstrate at least three years of technical experience in animal studies, particularly microsurgical techniques on rodent, rabbit, or pig models. Additional qualifications include proficiency in tissue sectioning, immunohistochemistry, neuro-electrophysiology, and related techniques, along with strong self-motivation and responsibility. The role is initially offered on a one-year renewable contract contingent on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
department: Department of Geography and Resource Management
description: This postdoctoral position involves conducting high-quality research on urban land use change modelling, climate change adaptation, smart cities, or urban informatics across China, Hong Kong, and the Greater Bay Area, with responsibilities including research execution, proposal development, and publication of findings. Candidates must hold a Ph.D. in Human Geography, Urban Studies/Planning, GIS, or related fields, demonstrate strong publication records and independent research capability, and possess advanced proficiency in spatial data analysis tools including ArcGIS, R, and Python. Experience with cellular automata modelling, agent-based modelling, or GeoAI is advantageous, along with fluent English communication skills and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively. The appointment is a one-year contract position beginning July 2025, renewable based on performance, with applications reviewed on a rolling basis.

title: Research Associate / Research Assistant
description: A Research Associate or Research Assistant position is available in a biomedical sciences research group focusing on cellular studies and functional characterization of disease-causing genes. Applicants must hold a Master's degree or above in biomedical research or a related field; Research Associates additionally require at least four years of post-qualification experience. Experience in stem cell culture and CRISPR/Cas9 genome editing is particularly valued. The role is offered on an initial one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance, and candidates should submit a curriculum vitae and reference letter through the online application portal.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in the School of Life Sciences involves participating in a butterfly-related research project, with responsibilities including conducting field and molecular biology experiments, drafting reports, collaborating with team members, and providing administrative support. Applicants require a Bachelor's degree or higher in Biology or a related field, bilingual proficiency in Chinese and English, and demonstrated self-motivation and organizational skills, though laboratory experience is advantageous and fresh graduates are encouraged to apply. The role is initially contracted for one year with potential renewal, and on-the-job training will be provided. Applications must be submitted online through the institutional career portal.

title: Full-time/Part-time Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences
description: The Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences seeks full-time or part-time research assistants to support clinical and research programmes focusing on ophthalmological conditions including glaucoma, diabetic retinopathy, and dry eye disease, with particular emphasis on Chinese medicine treatment approaches. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's degree in Chinese Medicine, Medicine, Public Health, Statistics, or related life sciences disciplines, demonstrate proficiency in English and Cantonese, and possess strong analytical and interpersonal skills; preference is given to registered Chinese Medicine Practitioners with relevant clinical or laboratory experience. Responsibilities include conducting literature reviews, managing and analysing data, assisting with laboratory work including specimen handling, and providing logistic support for clinical studies across multiple eye disease areas. Positions are offered initially on a one- to two-year renewable contract basis, with on-the-job training provided and potential shift work required.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s)
description: The position offers postdoctoral or research associate roles within a neuroscience laboratory focused on RNA regulation of brain circuit formation, maintenance, and plasticity using advanced techniques including 2-photon imaging, optogenetics, and whole-cell electrophysiology. Candidates must hold a PhD in a life science discipline, have strong publication records, and demonstrate expertise in electrophysiology and/or advanced imaging, with additional skills in molecular biology, bioinformatics, or microscopy optics being particularly valuable. Core responsibilities include conducting experiments and analysis, presenting findings at conferences, writing manuscripts, preparing research documents, and mentoring team members in a collaborative research environment. The position requires excellent communication skills, proficiency in English, and a commitment to high-quality neuroscience research, with applications submitted through an online portal including CV, transcripts, and statement of interest.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: The position is a Research Assistant role supporting a neuroscience laboratory investigating RNA regulation of brain circuit formation and plasticity using advanced techniques including two-photon imaging, optogenetics, and electrophysiology. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in life sciences with prior research experience, strong communication and organizational skills, and fluency in English and Chinese, with additional expertise in molecular biology, neuroscience, electrophysiology, or microscopy being highly valued. Responsibilities encompass laboratory support tasks including solution preparation, animal colony management, equipment maintenance, budget administration, reagent ordering, and assistance with research documentation. The position offers a supportive research environment with mentorship for career development within a top-ranked institution.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Assistant(s)
description: The Department of Electronic Engineering seeks Postdoctoral Fellows and Research Assistants to support development of durable, scalable smart window technology within an interdisciplinary materials and devices research group. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in Chemical Engineering, Electronic Engineering, Materials Engineering, or related fields, with a strong research record, independent work capability, and excellent communication skills; prior experience in smart materials, light/heat management, or industrial applications is preferred.
