Key responsibilities include assisting with smart window design and development, structural optimization, prototype fabrication and testing, experimental work, and collaborative laboratory research. The position is contract-based for one year with potential renewal based on performance.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This position is a Research Assistant or Junior Research Assistant in the School of Life Sciences, supporting plant research focused on sustainable agriculture and food security. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Chemistry, Molecular Biology, Plant Biology, or related fields, with strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and ability to work both independently and collaboratively; prior laboratory experience is advantageous. Key responsibilities include conducting analytical analyses and molecular experiments, assisting with project design and execution, collecting and interpreting experimental data, maintaining accurate records, cultivating plant materials, and staying current with scientific literature. Applicants should submit a detailed curriculum vitae and academic certificates through the online application portal.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Assistant
department: Institute for Tissue Engineering and Regenerative Medicine
description: This position offers Research Assistant and Postdoctoral Fellow roles in a laboratory specializing in tendon and bone tissue engineering, with focus areas including extracellular matrix-based biomaterials, stem cell and exosome therapies, and biomaterial development. The Research Assistant role requires a Bachelor's degree in biomedical sciences or engineering with strong organizational and communication skills, while the Postdoctoral Fellow position requires a Doctoral degree and the ability to work independently while demonstrating self-motivation and leadership capacity. Key responsibilities include conducting research experiments, managing laboratory operations, and training new staff members. Appointments are initially offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement, with fluency in both English and Chinese required for both positions.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
description: The Department of Biomedical Engineering seeks postdoctoral fellows to conduct interdisciplinary research on brain structure and function under Prof. Sadia Shakil, involving neuroimaging data analysis and computational model development. Applicants must hold a PhD in a relevant field (engineering, computer science, mathematics, or related disciplines) with strong foundations in mathematics and programming, particularly MATLAB and Python; prior experience with neuroimaging (MRI, fMRI, EEG) and machine learning/AI is advantageous. The role is initially a one-year contract position, renewable based on performance, with applications requiring a CV and statement of interest demonstrating alignment between the candidate's skills and position requirements. The department is highly ranked globally and emphasizes inter- and multi-disciplinary research excellence.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associates(s)
description: The Department of Electronic Engineering is recruiting Postdoctoral Fellow(s) or Research Associate(s) to conduct research on deep learning and remote sensing, with a focus on creating high-definition maps of Hong Kong using LiDAR, surround-view imagery, and GNSS data. Candidates must hold a PhD in Computer Science, Electronic Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or related fields, with demonstrated experience in research project development, manuscript preparation, data collection and analysis, and fluent English communication. Primary responsibilities include initiating and conducting research projects, collecting and analyzing data, contributing to technical reports and academic publications, and performing other duties assigned by the Principal Investigator. The position is offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: These research positions in electronic engineering seek candidates with a Bachelor's degree to assist with developing deep learning models, managing projects, and collecting/analyzing data within the department. Ideal applicants will demonstrate strong responsibility and attention to detail, analytical and interpersonal skills, bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively. Key responsibilities include training deep learning models, writing research reports, coordinating with collaborating parties, and performing additional assigned duties. The role is initially offered on a one-year renewable contract, with relevant work experience considered advantageous.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Senior Research Assistant(s) (External Fund) / Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Sports Science and Physical Education
description: The Department of Sports Science and Physical Education seeks Postdoctoral Fellows, Research Associates, Senior Research Assistants, or Research Assistants (depending on qualifications) to support externally funded research projects. Candidates must hold a PhD in Physical Education, Sports Science, Exercise Science, or related fields (for Postdoctoral Fellow level) or equivalent lower qualifications with relevant work experience, along with fluency in English and Chinese and strong communication skills; experience working with persons with disabilities in school or community settings is advantageous. Primary responsibilities include liaising with schools, NGOs, and vendors; planning and coordinating project activities; conducting impact assessments; analyzing research data; and supervising junior staff and students. Positions are offered on a one-year renewable contract basis subject to satisfactory performance.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s)/ Research Assistant(s)
description: Positions are available for Postdoctoral Fellows, Research Associates, and Research Assistants within the Department of Information Engineering's advanced wireless networks laboratory, focusing on wireless networks, stochastic control, and AI/ML applications. Postdoctoral and Associate-level candidates must hold a PhD in a relevant discipline with a demonstrated research track record, while Assistant-level candidates require a Bachelor's degree with strong academic performance and related research experience; hands-on experience with 5G/WiFi systems is advantageous. Appointees will conduct research in the specified areas, with senior-level staff also supervising graduate and undergraduate students. Initial contracts are for one year and renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Research Associate / Research Assistant
description: The Department of Psychiatry seeks a Research Associate or Research Assistant to support computational neuroscience projects translating systems neuroscience insights into clinical applications, with a focus on analyzing brain-wide neural datasets to study circuit-based neuropathological conditions in animal models and humans. Candidates should hold at least a Bachelor's degree in computational neuroscience, quantitative disciplines, or related fields (with a Master's degree and four years' experience preferred for the Associate role), demonstrate strong programming skills for analyzing neural and behavioral data, and possess proficiency in English and research communication. Key responsibilities include data analysis and modeling, developing analytical methodologies, disseminating findings, and collaborating with internal and external research teams. The position offers on-the-job training, international exposure, publication opportunities, and career development prospects on an initial one-year contract basis.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Research Assistant(s)
description: The Gerald Choa Neuroscience Institute seeks Postdoctoral Fellows, Research Associates, or Research Assistants to conduct auditory neuroscience research, including studies on perception, processing, pathology, and cochlear implants. Candidates must hold a PhD in Neuroscience or related fields, possess strong coding skills (Python or MATLAB), advanced signal processing expertise, and willingness to work with both human participants and animal models, with experience in electrophysiology, functional imaging, or psychoacoustic studies preferred. Responsibilities include designing and executing experiments, analyzing data, collaborating across teams, and mentoring junior researchers. The position offers a multidisciplinary environment with access to state-of-the-art facilities and opportunities for progression toward independent research roles.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: The School of Architecture seeks Research Assistants or Junior Research Assistants to support architectural and multidisciplinary research projects in Hong Kong. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree or higher in Architecture, Landscape Architecture, Design, or related fields, with proficiency in English and Chinese, advanced skills in CAD and design software, and demonstrated interest in interdisciplinary research bridging humanities and life sciences. Responsibilities include executing research projects, managing project administration and stakeholder engagement, producing architectural drawings and models, preparing reports, conducting field investigations, and supporting design and restoration initiatives. The position offers a one-year renewable contract, with applicants required to submit a statement of interest, CV, and design portfolio.

title: Research Assistant
department: Centre for Clinical Research and Biostatistics
description: This Research Assistant position supports the Centre for Clinical Research and Biostatistics in coordinating clinical research and data management projects with both local and international scope, particularly focused on pharmaceutical and medical device development. The role requires a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Science, strong organisational and project management skills, and proficiency in English, with preferred knowledge of statistics and programming. Key responsibilities include administrative project support, assistance with clinical research initiatives, involvement in protocol and case report form development, data management, and multi-centre study coordination. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract basis.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: This postdoctoral position in the Multimedia Communications Laboratory seeks a researcher with a PhD in a relevant discipline and demonstrated research experience to conduct independent research in mobile networking, Internet protocols, and machine learning. The role combines research responsibilities with co-supervision of graduate and undergraduate students. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract with the possibility of renewal based on mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position focuses on investigating gene regulation in skeletal muscle stem cells, muscle regeneration, and aging within an orthopaedic research group. Candidates must hold a PhD in Biology, Biomedical Science, Bioinformatics, or related fields obtained within the last five years, with a strong publication record and demonstrated ability to work independently and lead others. Responsibilities include conducting experiments and computational analyses, interpreting research findings, contributing to grant proposals and manuscripts, and mentoring junior group members. The role offers a one-year renewable contract.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s)
description: This position seeks postdoctoral fellows or research associates to conduct interdisciplinary research on brain structure and function under Dr. Sadia Shakil in a top-ranked biomedical engineering department. Candidates must hold a PhD in a relevant field (or a Master's degree plus four years of work experience) from a recognized institution, with strong foundations in mathematics, statistics, programming (MATLAB/Python), neuroimaging techniques (MRI/fMRI/EEG), and machine learning/AI. The role emphasizes analysis and model development combining multiple disciplines, and applicants should demonstrate strong English communication and technical writing abilities. Positions are available immediately on renewable one-year contracts.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: The position is for a Research Assistant supporting a large-scale AI education initiative aimed at integrating artificial intelligence into Hong Kong secondary school curricula. Core responsibilities include developing bilingual educational materials, creating hands-on courseware using programming languages and AI frameworks, and delivering training to schools and students. Applicants must hold a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, AI, Data Science or related fields, with strong English communication skills; desirable qualifications include AI expertise, Python proficiency, teaching experience, and Chinese language abilities. The role is offered initially on a two-year renewable contract basis.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in orthopaedics involves clinical trial research, sample management, and project coordination at a hospital setting, with particular focus on sports medicine and osteoarthritis studies. Candidates should hold a Master's or Bachelor's degree in Biomedical Sciences, Biology, or related fields, with demonstrated research experience and strong English and Cantonese communication skills. Key responsibilities include managing clinical sample tissue banks, obtaining research ethics approvals, preparing manuscripts, and coordinating projects, with prior experience in blood-taking and clinical data analysis being advantageous. The role is initially contracted for one year and renewable based on performance, requiring applicants to submit a comprehensive CV, academic certificates, and reference letters.

title: Senior Research Assistant(s) (External Fund) / Research Assistant(s)
description: The School of Architecture seeks two research assistants to join a team studying Hong Kong's urban spaces through data-driven methods, focusing on public space activity patterns, housing estate improvements, and elderly populations in dense urban areas. Candidates must hold a Master's degree in Architecture, Urban Design, Urban Analytics, or Computer Science with four years of relevant experience (or less for the Research Assistant role), alongside strong proficiency in 3D modelling (Rhino3D, Grasshopper, Python), written English, and teamworking skills. The Senior Research Assistant will lead development of Computer Vision analysis methods, manage data collection and processing, and conduct computational analysis and reporting, while the Research Assistant will handle site coordination, create digital models and 2D/3D representations of housing estates, and perform spatial analysis. The positions offer significant career development support including research skills training, co-authored publications, and exposure to international conferences and teaching opportunities for those interested in pursuing academic careers or

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Centre for Cardiovascular Genomics and Medicine
description: This postdoctoral position involves conducting basic and clinical research in cardiovascular diseases with a focus on translational discoveries and precision medicine development within a dedicated genomics centre. The successful candidate will hold a PhD in biomedical science, bioinformatics, statistics, or related fields, with proficiency in both English and Cantonese, and ideally possess prior experience in genetics, genomics, statistics, or bioinformatics. The role requires strong teamwork and responsibility, and is initially offered on a one-year contract basis with potential renewal depending on performance. The position is distinctive for its emphasis on bridging research discoveries with clinical applications in cardiovascular medicine.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This full-time postdoctoral position in the Research Centre for Translation focuses on Chinese translation history and requires a PhD in this field or a closely related discipline, along with a strong research track record and demonstrated capacity for collaborative work. Primary responsibilities include publishing in international journals, supporting the Centre director's research initiatives, organizing academic events, and building disciplinary networks to foster collaboration. Candidates must submit a resume, cover letter, academic credentials, and at least one reference letter through the online application portal, which remains open until the position is filled. The initial one-year contract is renewable based on performance and mutual agreement, with the start date flexible and commencing as soon as possible.

title: Research Fellow
description: This Research Fellow position in the Department of Electronic Engineering seeks a highly experienced researcher specializing in antenna engineering, microwave engineering, radar systems, or synthetic aperture radars, with a Ph.D. and at least eight years of postqualification experience in these fields. The successful candidate will conduct independent, top-tier research under professorial supervision, contribute to postgraduate student mentoring, and demonstrate excellence through publications and a strong academic record. Key requirements include fluency in English, organizational skills, self-motivation, and proven expertise preferably in antenna engineering or synthetic aperture radar systems. The role is offered initially on a one-year contract renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: Joint CUHK-NTEC Clinical Research Management Office
description: This Research Assistant position supports clinical research operations at a hospital research centre, requiring a bachelor's degree in biological science or health-related fields and fluent English and Chinese communication skills. Key responsibilities include organizing clinical research projects, coordinating studies, collecting and managing clinical data, performing site monitoring and start-up activities, and providing general administrative support. The role particularly values strong interpersonal skills, responsibility, self-motivation, and MS Office proficiency, with clinical research experience preferred but not mandatory. The appointment is a one-year renewable contract position.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
description: Postdoctoral fellows are sought to join a pharmacy research team, developing natural language processing algorithms to extract content from Chinese and English articles. Candidates must hold a PhD in Pharmacy, Computer Science, or related fields from a reputable institution, with proficiency in at least one programming language and fluent English and Chinese communication skills. Key responsibilities include programming algorithm implementation, leading research projects, securing external funding, training team members, and preparing research documentation. The position offers a one-year renewable contract contingent on performance.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
department: Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences
description: The Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences seeks postdoctoral fellows to conduct clinical and translational research across multiple eye disease areas including retinal, corneal, glaucoma, and genetic conditions, working within a network of over 200 ophthalmologists and visual scientists. Candidates must hold a PhD in Biological Sciences (preferably ophthalmology or cancer research), demonstrate proficiency in molecular biology and cell culture techniques, and possess strong independent research capabilities alongside collaborative skills. Primary responsibilities include coordinating research projects, analyzing data, preparing manuscripts and grant applications, and mentoring junior researchers and students. The position is offered on a one-to-two-year renewable contract, with experience in next-generation sequencing considered advantageous.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: Centre for University and School Partnership
description: Applicants for this research position should hold a Bachelor's degree in Social Sciences or education-related fields, demonstrate bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese for academic writing, and possess strong organisational and interpersonal skills alongside knowledge of Hong Kong's educational landscape. The role involves assisting with research and evaluation of centre projects, compiling research papers and reports, and providing general project support, with opportunities available for both experienced researchers and junior candidates. The position is offered on an initial one-year contract basis, renewable subject to funding and performance. Candidates must submit a one-page curriculum vitae and a one-page cover letter detailing their research experience and aspirations through the online application portal.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: This position offers Research Assistant or Junior Research Assistant roles within the School of Life Sciences, focused on marine and coral conservation education. Applicants require a Bachelor's degree in Biology, Environmental Science, Marine Science, or related fields with preferably 1-2 years of work experience (though fresh graduates and Associate degree holders are welcome), along with proficiency in Microsoft Office, fluent spoken and written Chinese and English, and strong interpersonal skills. Primary responsibilities include organizing and delivering educational programs for secondary schools, coordinating workshops and conservation activities, liaising with external organizations, managing program budgets, creating educational materials, and overseeing social media outreach related to marine conservation. The position is offered initially on a one-year renewable contract with occasional weekend and outdoor work required.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
description: Multiple postdoctoral positions are available in electronic engineering to conduct research on smart materials and devices, with focus areas including fabrication techniques, thermal management, advanced sensing and camouflage technologies, flexible electronics, and soft robotics. Candidates must hold a PhD from a top 100 STEM institution with background in Materials Science, Engineering, Energy, or related fields, along with demonstrated strong analytical and writing skills, excellent academic records, and hands-on experience in materials fabrication. Applicants should be highly self-motivated team players with proven recognition from leading institutions. The initial one-year contract is renewable based on mutual agreement, with applications requiring submission of CV, transcripts, representative publications, and demonstration projects.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Sports Science and Physical Education
description: This full-time research assistant position requires a Bachelor's degree or higher in Physical Education, Sports Science, Exercise Science, Rehabilitation, Public Health, or related fields, along with fluency in written and spoken English and Chinese. The role involves recruiting school participants, collecting and analyzing research data, organizing and delivering physical activity interventions and workshops, and supporting other departmental tasks. Strong communication skills and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively are essential, with prior experience working with children and youth with special educational needs being advantageous. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract, renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Full-time/Part-time Research Associate
department: Hong Kong Institute of Asia-Pacific Studies
description: This role involves supervising a team of telephone interviewers at a survey research laboratory with 60 workstations, overseeing data collection operations during evening shifts (2:30–10:30 pm for full-time or 6:30–10:30 pm for part-time positions). Candidates must hold a Master's degree, possess at least ten years of relevant post-qualification experience, demonstrate fluency in Cantonese and spoken English, and exhibit strong problem-solving and organisational abilities, with survey management experience preferred. The position is initially offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance and mutual agreement, and supports the laboratory's mission of providing professional survey services to researchers and conducting public opinion polls.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position involves conducting experiments on cardiomyocyte differentiation, heart development, and regeneration using cultured mammalian cells, zebrafish, and mouse models, alongside adeno-associated virus work under the supervision of a Principal Investigator. Applicants must hold a Bachelor's degree in Biomedical Sciences or a related field and demonstrate practical laboratory experience in molecular and cellular biology and biochemistry, along with strong communication skills and proficiency with standard laboratory equipment. Desirable qualifications include recent hands-on lab experience, molecular biology expertise, CRISPR/Cas9 competency, and familiarity with zebrafish handling. The role offers an initial one-year contract position with potential renewal contingent on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position focuses on investigating cardiomyocyte differentiation, heart development, and regeneration using cultured mammalian cells, zebrafish, and mouse models, with applications of adeno-associated viruses. Candidates must hold a Ph.D. in Biomedical Sciences or related fields and demonstrate strong laboratory expertise in molecular and cellular biology, with desirable skills including CRISPR/Cas9 proficiency and experience in mouse or zebrafish handling. The fellow will design and execute research projects, present findings at conferences, prepare manuscripts for publication, and receive mentoring for independent grant applications while working closely with the supervising Principal Investigator. The appointment is offered initially on a one-year contract basis, commencing immediately and renewable based on performance.

title: Associate Professor(s)/Assistant Professor(s)
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: The Department of Accountancy seeks Associate or Assistant Professors with strong research credentials and balanced expertise in research, teaching, and professional engagement across accounting disciplines, with preference for candidates specializing in corporate governance, sustainability, auditing, or assurance. Applicants must hold a PhD and professional accounting certification, and will be expected to contribute to undergraduate and postgraduate teaching, conduct impactful research, and engage with the accounting profession. The position offers opportunities to teach in a liberal-arts-oriented institution with AACSB-accredited business school status, small interactive classes, and strong emphasis on student development. Candidates should demonstrate commitment to both academic excellence and practical application of accounting knowledge within a supportive institutional environment focused on quality education and regional impact.

title: Associate Professor/Assistant Professor (Full-time)
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This position is a full-time Associate or Assistant Professor role in the Department of Economics and Finance, requiring applicants to hold a PhD in Finance, Economics, or a related discipline with demonstrated teaching and research experience at tertiary level. Key responsibilities include developing and delivering specialist courses across finance, economics, real estate, investment, and emerging areas like FinTech and ESG, while contributing to programme development and conducting research. Candidates should have strong publication records, international academic exposure, and subject expertise; those applying for the Real Estate Development specialization with RICS chartered membership are particularly preferred. The position is distinctive for its focus on a regional liberal-arts institution emphasizing undergraduate education alongside research, with strong institutional recognition including AACSB accreditation and top regional rankings.

title: Lecturer
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This Lecturer position in Economics and Finance requires a PhD holder with strong teaching experience at undergraduate and postgraduate levels and a demonstrated research record in finance, economics, or related disciplines. Key responsibilities include designing and delivering modules across finance, economics, real estate, investment, and emerging areas like FinTech and ESG, along with contributing to programme development, conducting research, and supporting student development. The role is distinctive for its positioning within a regionally top-ranked liberal arts university that emphasizes both professional excellence and critical thinking, with distinctive features including small-class teaching, close student-teacher relationships, and integration within a residential college system. Candidates with international exposure and publications are particularly preferred.

title: Associate Professor / Assistant Professor (Full-time)
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: The Department of Management seeks full-time Associate Professor or Assistant Professor positions requiring a PhD degree and strong research credentials. Key responsibilities include teaching across undergraduate and postgraduate levels, conducting impactful research, and contributing to the department's scholarly reputation within a liberal-arts-oriented business school context. The role offers the opportunity to join a rapidly ascending private institution in Hong Kong that has achieved prestigious international accreditations (AACSB, pursuing EQUIS) and consistently ranks among Asia's top business schools. Applicants should be prepared to engage in both traditional academic responsibilities and the institution's distinctive emphasis on student mentorship and interdisciplinary collaboration within a residential college environment.

title: Associate Professor / Assistant Professor (Full-time)
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: The Department of Management at a leading Hong Kong liberal arts university seeks full-time Associate or Assistant Professors with strong research credentials in macro-level management fields including International Business, Strategic Management, Entrepreneurship, or Sustainability. Candidates must hold a PhD in a related discipline, demonstrate an excellent publication record in top-tier journals, possess tertiary-level teaching experience, and show commitment to student engagement and scholarly activities. The position offers competitive salary commensurate with qualifications, with an anticipated start date of January 2026. This appointment is distinctive for its emphasis on both research excellence and teaching quality within a university known for its innovative liberal arts model, AACSB-accredited business school, and strong focus on undergraduate education and student-faculty relationships.

title: Assistant Professor (Full-time)
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This full-time Assistant Professor position in Marketing seeks a PhD-qualified scholar with a strong publication record in top-tier journals and demonstrated teaching experience at the tertiary level. The role requires candidates to teach across diverse marketing modules, conduct research with publication aspirations in world-leading journals, and actively engage in service to students and stakeholders. Applicants must show strong commitment to both scholarly activities and student engagement, with the position offering a start date of January or August 2026 and salary based on qualifications and experience.

title: Assistant Professor
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
department: Department of Mathematics, Statistics and Insurance
description: This is an Assistant Professor position in the Department of Mathematics, Statistics and Insurance focused on teaching Data Science, Actuarial Science, Insurance, and Risk Management courses at both undergraduate and postgraduate levels. The role requires a doctoral degree and a strong research track record with publications in leading peer-reviewed journals in relevant disciplines such as Statistics, Applied Mathematics, or Actuarial Science. Beyond teaching and research, the position involves fostering industry connections through professional talks and events, and contributing to departmental administration. The distinctive aspect of this role is its emphasis on bridging academic expertise with professional practice, positioning faculty to enhance students' awareness of industry developments and career opportunities in actuarial and financial risk sectors.

title: Senior Lecturer / Lecturer
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This position involves planning and delivering Social Science courses across multiple levels (undergraduate, postgraduate, and general education), while contributing to programme development, research, and administrative duties within the department. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in Political Science, Sociology, or East Asian Studies with solid grounding in Social Science research methods and demonstrated experience in tertiary teaching. Strong administrative experience in higher education is essential, along with the ability to teach courses on global affairs and social sustainability, particularly addressing urbanisation, international relations, and related societal themes in Asian and global contexts. The role offers the opportunity to join a highly-ranked liberal-arts institution with recognition for research excellence and innovative undergraduate education in the region.

title: Associate Professor / Assistant Professor (Full-time)
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
department: Department of Supply Chain and Information Management
description: This full-time position in the Department of Supply Chain and Information Management seeks an Associate or Assistant Professor to teach specialist modules, contribute to programme development, conduct research, and fulfill administrative duties. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in a related field with strong expertise in areas such as Management Science, Supply Chain Management, Transportation, or Aviation, along with proven teaching and research experience at tertiary level and a solid publication record. Preference is given to applicants with industry experience and proficiency in both English and Chinese. The role emphasizes the institution's commitment to research-informed teaching within a liberal arts framework focused on student-centered education and community engagement.

title: Part-time Instructor(s) (Action Learning and Experiential Teaching)
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: The position is a part-time instructor role within the School of Business focused on action learning and experiential teaching methodologies. The instructor will be responsible for designing and delivering interactive, experience-based courses that develop students' practical skills and critical thinking within the liberal arts-professional education model. Ideal candidates should possess expertise in experiential pedagogy and demonstrate ability to facilitate active learning environments that prepare students for professional practice. This role is distinctive given the School's recent AACSB accreditation, pursuit of EQUIS accreditation, and commitment to innovative teaching approaches that distinguish it as a leading business school in Asia.

title: Assistant Professor
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This Assistant Professor position in the School of Transdisciplinary Studies involves developing and teaching interdisciplinary programmes, preparing accreditation documents, and fostering cross-disciplinary collaboration across the institution. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in health studies, social policy, higher education leadership, or related fields, with substantial higher education teaching experience and a proven track record in programme development, publications, and research. The role is distinctive for its focus on transdisciplinary rather than traditional disciplinary work, requiring candidates who can bridge multiple fields and demonstrate collaborative leadership. Successful applicants should be self-motivated scholars committed to student engagement and institutional service in advancing innovative, integrated approaches to education.

title: Associate Professor / Assistant Professor
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
department: School of Translation and Foreign Languages
description: The position is an Associate or Assistant Professor role in the School of Translation and Foreign Languages, requiring applicants to hold a doctoral degree in translation studies or related field and demonstrate proven teaching experience at tertiary level. Primary responsibilities include delivering undergraduate and postgraduate teaching, developing course materials and assessments, providing academic advising, supervising student projects, and contributing to programme quality assurance and university governance. The role also demands active engagement in scholarly research and publication, along with participation in external engagement and student recruitment activities. This appointment is distinctive in being located within a regionally-ranked liberal-arts institution that emphasizes small-class teaching, close student-teacher relationships, and a blend of professional and liberal education.

title: Manager (Facility Management Services)
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This role manages facility management services for a growing private liberal-arts university with over 7,000 students across six schools. The Manager will oversee campus facilities and operations within the Campus Development and Management Office, supporting the institution's commitment to maintaining award-winning green campus infrastructure and high-quality student support services. The position requires expertise in facility management and the ability to align operational excellence with the university's "Liberal + Professional" educational model and sustainability priorities. This is a key operational role in a regionally recognized institution that has recently achieved AACSB accreditation and consistently ranks among top universities in Asia for quality education and research impact.

title: Director of School of Transdisciplinary Studies
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: The Director of the School of Transdisciplinary Studies will lead one of six schools at a Hong Kong-based liberal arts university serving over 7,000 students. The role requires overseeing academic programme development, faculty management, and student support within a transdisciplinary framework that emphasises critical thinking, innovation, and social responsibility. The successful candidate should demonstrate strong leadership in advancing research impact, fostering collaborative teaching approaches, and aligning the school's offerings with the institution's "Liberal + Professional" educational model and its established reputation for excellence in employability and community engagement.

title: Security Superintendent
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This position requires a Security Superintendent to oversee all campus security operations, including staff scheduling, access control systems, CCTV monitoring, emergency preparedness coordination, and incident investigation within a liberal arts university setting. The ideal candidate should hold an accredited diploma or degree in Security Management or related field, possess a QAS certificate and security personnel permit, and bring at least 8 years of security operations experience with a minimum of 5 years in a supervisory capacity. Key competencies include expertise in security systems, emergency response, risk management, relevant legal knowledge, and strong leadership and communication skills. The role encompasses budget management, policy development and enforcement, and liaison with law enforcement and emergency services to maintain campus safety.

title: Executive Officer
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This Executive Officer position in the Registry department involves coordinating student admission and recruitment activities, liaising with schools and external stakeholders, and managing recruitment data and statistics. The successful candidate must hold a bachelor's degree with at least 3 years of relevant experience, preferably in student recruitment and promotional events, and demonstrate proficiency in English and Chinese (both Cantonese and Putonghua) along with strong computer skills. The role includes secretarial duties for committees, report preparation, and general support for Registry functions. This position is distinctive in its focus on international student recruitment for a regionally-ranked private liberal arts university with a strong emphasis on both professional and liberal education.

title: Associate Vice-President (Quality Assurance)
description: Associate Vice-President (Quality Assurance). Please visit the application link for full details.

title: Director
description: The position requires a director to lead the Information Technology Services Centre with extensive managerial experience in higher education administration, ideally gained from a similar role in local or international institutions. Candidates must hold a postgraduate qualification, with a doctorate preferred, and demonstrate at least five years of relevant senior-level administrative experience. Fluency in both English and Chinese, in written and spoken forms, is essential. This role is distinctive in requiring both advanced academic credentials and proven leadership experience specifically within higher education IT service environments.

title: [Full-time / Part-time] Associate Professors / Assistant Professors / Senior Lecturers
department: School of Business and Hospitality Management
description: The position involves teaching across multiple levels (sub-degree through postgraduate) in the School of Business and Hospitality Management, along with research activities and administrative responsibilities for programmes or courses. Candidates for Assistant Professor roles must hold a PhD in a relevant discipline and demonstrate teaching experience, with preference given to those with strong undergraduate/postgraduate teaching records, programme management experience, and established research credentials. Senior Lecturer applicants require a Master's degree and professional membership, while all candidates must possess strong English proficiency, preferably with Chinese language skills, and at least three years of supervisory or managerial industry experience. Higher appointment levels are possible for candidates with particularly strong research records and extensive experience.

title: Research Professor
description: This Research Professor position requires leading the institution's research agenda and scholarly activities while maintaining alignment with strategic university goals. The role demands an established scholar with a PhD, extensive publication record at professorial level, and a demonstrated ability to secure major competitive research funding and produce internationally recognized research output. Key responsibilities include mentoring junior faculty and postdoctoral researchers, initiating collaborations with domestic and international institutions, and advising on academic programme quality and staff performance evaluation. The ideal candidate will bring strong professional networks in industry and education, excellent management skills, and a proven leadership track record in research excellence within the university's priority research themes.

title: Assistant Professors/ Senior Lecturers/ Lecturers
department: S.K. Yee School of Health Sciences
description: The position involves teaching and research in pharmaceutical dispensing within health sciences at the lecturer, senior lecturer, or assistant professor level. Candidates must hold a Master's degree in a relevant discipline, with strong preference for those possessing a Doctoral degree and prior experience teaching in tertiary programmes. Fluency in both English and Chinese is essential. The role emphasises expertise in pharmaceutical dispensing and commitment to academic development.

title: [Full-time/ Part-time] Assistant Professor/ Lecturer
department: Yam Pak Charitable Foundation School of Computing and Information Sciences
description: This position requires a computing and information sciences academic to deliver teaching across undergraduate and postgraduate programmes while contributing to curriculum development and review. The role demands a strong research focus, with expectation of publications in top-tier journals, alongside engagement in consultancy and scholarly activities. Additional academic and administrative responsibilities may be assigned by the Dean as needed.

title: Fieldwork Coordinator cum Lecturer/ Senior Lecturer
department: Felizberta Lo Padilla Tong School of Social Sciences
description: The appointee will be responsible (i) to co-ordinate fieldwork placement; (ii) to liaise with agency for fieldwork places; (iii) to convene fieldwork co-ordination other related meetings; and (iv) to perform teaching and related duties.

title: [Full-time/Part-time] Assistant Professors / Senior Lecturers / Lecturers
department: Felizberta Lo Padilla Tong School of Social Sciences
description: This position invites applications for academic roles (Assistant Professor, Senior Lecturer, or Lecturer level) within a social sciences school. Candidates must hold a Master's degree in a relevant discipline and demonstrate teaching or professional experience, though priority will be given to those with doctoral qualifications and evidence of teaching at degree level or strong research records. Professional registration in Hong Kong or internationally is a mandatory requirement for the specific discipline.

title: [Full-time/Part-time] Professors / Associate Professors
department: Felizberta Lo Padilla Tong School of Social Sciences
description: The position involves teaching responsibilities across degree and sub-degree level programmes within the School of Social Sciences. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in a relevant discipline and demonstrate substantial teaching or professional experience, with preference given to those with degree-level teaching experience or a strong research record. Professional registration in Hong Kong or an equivalent overseas jurisdiction is a mandatory requirement for the specific discipline. This is a full-time or part-time appointment at either Professor or Associate Professor level.

title: Part-time Research Assistant
description: This part-time research assistant role supports a humanities research project with responsibilities spanning administrative coordination, data collection and preprocessing, participant recruitment, data annotation, dataset management, and report preparation. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in a humanities field and demonstrate solid research experience alongside strong communication and teamwork capabilities. Bilingual proficiency in Chinese and English, both written and spoken, is essential, along with competent computer skills. The position values collaborative work ethic and the ability to manage multiple research support tasks efficiently.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position supports a five-year psychosocial study examining service outcomes for students with special educational needs, involving literature review, interview coordination and analysis, data analysis, report compilation, and administrative support. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree or higher in education, applied research, psychology, statistics, or social work, with demonstrated experience in quantitative and/or qualitative research methods. The role requires proficiency in conducting in-depth interviews and analyzing quantitative data, alongside strong independent work capabilities, attention to detail, and analytical thinking. Fluency in English and Chinese, combined with excellent communication skills, is essential.

title: Senior Research Associate
department: School of Computing and Information Sciences
description: The role involves supporting a research project on crane stability monitoring using digital twin and IoT technologies, with primary responsibilities including conducting interviews and focus groups, collecting and analyzing quantitative data, and preparing operational documentation and reports. Candidates must hold at least a Bachelor's degree (Master's or MPhil preferred) in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related fields, with demonstrated experience in statistical data analysis and the ability to work independently. Strong communication, interpersonal, and English writing skills are essential, and prior publication experience is advantageous. This position is distinctive for its focus on applying emerging technologies to construction safety monitoring and its blend of qualitative research methods with quantitative data analysis.

title: Research Assistant
department: S School of Computing and Information Sciences
description: This Research Assistant position in computing and information sciences involves collecting and analyzing performance data for wet electrostatic precipitators while proposing design improvements, publishing findings in peer-reviewed journals, and presenting at conferences. The role requires close collaboration with the project lead and team members to achieve project objectives, along with mentoring responsibilities for undergraduate and graduate students. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related fields, with demonstrated experience in statistical data analysis, independent work capability, and strong communication and writing skills. Prior publication experience is preferred, making this position well-suited for someone seeking to establish or advance a research career.

title: Part-time Senior Research Assistant
department: Ip Ying To Lee Yu Yee School of Humanities and Languages
description: This part-time senior research assistant position involves comprehensive support for a research project, including data collection and preprocessing, statistical analysis, trend projection, data management, and report preparation. Candidates must hold a Master's degree, preferably interdisciplinary, with demonstrated research experience and a publication record. The role requires strong communication skills, proficiency in English and computer applications, and flexibility to work across diverse project materials.

title: Part-time Research Assistant
department: Rita Tong Liu School of Business and Hospitality Management
description: Three part-time research assistants will be appointed across distinct disciplinary areas—Accounting & Finance, Entrepreneurship & Management, and Hotel & Tourism & Marketing—each supporting research projects through literature reviews, data collection and analysis, database management, and report preparation. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree or higher with demonstrated research experience and proficiency in statistical and database tools such as SPSS, SQL, and MS Access. Strong communication skills, bilingual competency in English and Chinese (including Putonghua), and solid computer literacy are essential, alongside a collaborative attitude and positive work approach.
